3.content:
### How to Obtain
World-drop appearance from **Classic** content. Pieces drop from outdoor mobs, rare elites, and treasure chests across the expansion's zones. This is a recolor / lookalike variant — the items may share appearances with a base set from a different expansion, so they can drop in zones outside the listed expansion range.

### Farming Logistics
No lockout — farm continuously. Individual drop rates from any specific mob are low, so most collectors check the **Auction House** first (these pieces are bind-on-equip and often listed). Rare-elite hunts on Time-walking weeks or Cross-Realm group routes massively speed up the search.

### Farming Tips
Wearable by any class — appearance is shared across all armor types. Install Handynotes plus the relevant expansion's "Rares + Treasures" plugin to map every spawn. AoE classes (Frost Mage, Havoc DH, Outlaw Rogue) clear contested zones faster than single-target builds.

Wearable by any class — appearance is shared across all armor types. Most legacy sets at current max level are soloable; modern current-expansion sets may require active content engagement. Check the Wowhead set page for difficulty and source details.

If pieces are bind-on-equip, check the Auction House on your realm before farming directly. The Wardrobe automatically learns appearances on first loot pickup, so it's safe to roll Need even if you'll vendor the item later.

14.content:
### How to Obtain
Available from the **Trading Post**, located in Orgrimmar (Valley of Strength) or Stormwind (Trade District). Purchased with **Trader's Tender**, the currency earned by completing monthly Traveler's Log activities (about 500 Tender per month plus bonus activities).

### Farming Logistics
Trading Post inventory rotates **monthly** — if this set is not in the current month's catalog, it will eventually return in a future rotation. Tender carries over indefinitely between months, so you can save up for higher-priced items. No lockout once purchased.

### Farming Tips
Wearable by any class — appearance is shared across all armor types. Complete every Traveler's Log activity each month to maximize Tender — they cover all content (dungeons, raids, PvP, world quests, professions). Set bundles typically cost 500–750 Tender; individual pieces cost less.

52.content:
### How to Obtain
Available from the **Trading Post**, located in Orgrimmar (Valley of Strength) or Stormwind (Trade District). Purchased with **Trader's Tender**, the currency earned by completing monthly Traveler's Log activities (about 500 Tender per month plus bonus activities).

### Farming Logistics
Trading Post inventory rotates **monthly** — if this set is not in the current month's catalog, it will eventually return in a future rotation. Tender carries over indefinitely between months, so you can save up for higher-priced items. No lockout once purchased.

### Farming Tips
Wearable by any class — appearance is shared across all armor types. Complete every Traveler's Log activity each month to maximize Tender — they cover all content (dungeons, raids, PvP, world quests, professions). Set bundles typically cost 500–750 Tender; individual pieces cost less.

Feral Garb is a Classic appearance set. The exact source isn't categorized in our database — open the Wowhead link below for the canonical piece list and drop locations.

### Farming Logistics
Wearable by any class — appearance is shared across all armor types. Most legacy sets at current max level are soloable; modern current-expansion sets may require active content engagement. Check the Wowhead set page for difficulty and source details.

### Farming Tips
If pieces are bind-on-equip, check the Auction House on your realm before farming directly. The Wardrobe automatically learns appearances on first loot pickup, so it's safe to roll Need even if you'll vendor the item later.

115.content:
### How to Obtain
World-drop appearance from **Classic** content. Pieces drop from outdoor mobs, rare elites, and treasure chests across the expansion's zones. This is a recolor / lookalike variant — the items may share appearances with a base set from a different expansion, so they can drop in zones outside the listed expansion range.

### Farming Logistics
No lockout — farm continuously. Individual drop rates from any specific mob are low, so most collectors check the **Auction House** first (these pieces are bind-on-equip and often listed). Rare-elite hunts on Time-walking weeks or Cross-Realm group routes massively speed up the search.

### Farming Tips
Wearable by any class — appearance is shared across all armor types. Install Handynotes plus the relevant expansion's "Rares + Treasures" plugin to map every spawn. AoE classes (Frost Mage, Havoc DH, Outlaw Rogue) clear contested zones faster than single-target builds.

World-drop appearance from **Classic** content. Pieces drop from outdoor mobs, rare elites, and treasure chests across the expansion's zones. This is a recolor / lookalike variant — the items may share appearances with a base set from a different expansion, so they can drop in zones outside the listed expansion range.

### Farming Logistics
No lockout — farm continuously. Individual drop rates from any specific mob are low, so most collectors check the **Auction House** first (these pieces are bind-on-equip and often listed). Rare-elite hunts on Time-walking weeks or Cross-Realm group routes massively speed up the search.

### Farming Tips
Wearable by any class — appearance is shared across all armor types. Install Handynotes plus the relevant expansion's "Rares + Treasures" plugin to map every spawn. AoE classes (Frost Mage, Havoc DH, Outlaw Rogue) clear contested zones faster than single-target builds.

164.content:
### How to Obtain
Grand Garb is a Classic appearance set. The exact source isn't categorized in our database — open the Wowhead link below for the canonical piece list and drop locations.

### Farming Logistics
Leather armor, available to Rogue, Druid, Monk, Demon Hunter. Most legacy sets at current max level are soloable; modern current-expansion sets may require active content engagement. Check the Wowhead set page for difficulty and source details.

### Farming Tips
If pieces are bind-on-equip, check the Auction House on your realm before farming directly. The Wardrobe automatically learns appearances on first loot pickup, so it's safe to roll Need even if you'll vendor the item later.

Mail armor, available to Hunter, Shaman, Evoker. Install Handynotes plus the relevant expansion's "Rares + Treasures" plugin to map every spawn. AoE classes (Frost Mage, Havoc DH, Outlaw Rogue) clear contested zones faster than single-target builds.

World-drop appearance from **Classic** content. Pieces drop from outdoor mobs, rare elites, and treasure chests across the expansion's zones. This is a recolor / lookalike variant — the items may share appearances with a base set from a different expansion, so they can drop in zones outside the listed expansion range.

### Farming Logistics
No lockout — farm continuously. Individual drop rates from any specific mob are low, so most collectors check the **Auction House** first (these pieces are bind-on-equip and often listed). Rare-elite hunts on Time-walking weeks or Cross-Realm group routes massively speed up the search.

### Farming Tips
Wearable by any class — appearance is shared across all armor types. Install Handynotes plus the relevant expansion's "Rares + Treasures" plugin to map every spawn. AoE classes (Frost Mage, Havoc DH, Outlaw Rogue) clear contested zones faster than single-target builds.

262.content:
### How to Obtain
Ornate Mail is a Classic appearance set. The exact source isn't categorized in our database — open the Wowhead link below for the canonical piece list and drop locations.

### Farming Logistics
Mail armor, available to Hunter, Shaman, Evoker. Most legacy sets at current max level are soloable; modern current-expansion sets may require active content engagement. Check the Wowhead set page for difficulty and source details.

### Farming Tips
If pieces are bind-on-equip, check the Auction House on your realm before farming directly. The Wardrobe automatically learns appearances on first loot pickup, so it's safe to roll Need even if you'll vendor the item later.

331.content:
### How to Obtain
Drops from bosses inside **a Burning Crusade-era raid** (Burning Crusade). Each main armor slot (Head, Shoulders, Chest, Hands, Legs, Feet, Waist, Wrists) drops from a specific boss in the raid — open the set on Wowhead via the link below for the exact boss-to-slot mapping.

### Farming Logistics
Standard raid weekly lockout — the full instance resets every Tuesday. This is the Heroic difficulty version of the appearance. Other difficulties (LFR / Normal / Heroic / Mythic) use separate tints and are tracked as separate sets. At current max level the raid is usually soloable or trivially small-group, so you can collect missing pieces 1–2 weeks at a time.

### Farming Tips
Plate armor, available to Warrior, Paladin, Death Knight. Use the Great Vault and any bonus-roll currency from this expansion to maximize attempts per week. If a piece keeps not dropping, target-farm the specific boss and skip the rest.

This is a recolor / lookalike variant — the items may share appearances with a base set from a different expansion, so they can drop in zones outside the listed expansion range.

### Farming Logistics
No lockout — farm continuously. Individual drop rates from any specific mob are low, so most collectors check the **Auction House** first (these pieces are bind-on-equip and often listed). Rare-elite hunts on Time-walking weeks or Cross-Realm group routes massively speed up the search.

### Farming Tips
Wearable by any class — appearance is shared across all armor types. Install Handynotes plus the relevant expansion's "Rares + Treasures" plugin to map every spawn. AoE classes (Frost Mage, Havoc DH, Outlaw Rogue) clear contested zones faster than single-target builds.

413.content:
### How to Obtain
Drops from bosses inside **Sunwell** (Burning Crusade). Each main armor slot (Head, Shoulders, Chest, Hands, Legs, Feet, Waist, Wrists) drops from a specific boss in the raid — open the set on Wowhead via the link below for the exact boss-to-slot mapping.

### Farming Logistics
Standard raid weekly lockout — the full instance resets every Tuesday. Raid bosses have a chance to drop set pieces on each clear; difficulties (LFR / Normal / Heroic / Mythic) share the same items but render with different color tints. At current max level the raid is usually soloable or trivially small-group, so you can collect missing pieces 1–2 weeks at a time.

### Farming Tips
Cloth armor, available to Mage, Priest, Warlock. Use the Great Vault and any bonus-roll currency from this expansion to maximize attempts per week. If a piece keeps not dropping, target-farm the specific boss and skip the rest.

### Farming Logistics
Standard raid weekly lockout — the full instance resets every Tuesday. Raid bosses have a chance to drop set pieces on each clear; difficulties (LFR / Normal / Heroic / Mythic) share the same items but render with different color tints. At current max level the raid is usually soloable or trivially small-group, so you can collect missing pieces 1–2 weeks at a time.

### Farming Tips
Cloth armor, available to Mage, Priest, Warlock. Use the Great Vault and any bonus-roll currency from this expansion to maximize attempts per week. If a piece keeps not dropping, target-farm the specific boss and skip the rest.

422.content:
### How to Obtain
World-drop appearance from **Cataclysm** content. Pieces drop from outdoor mobs, rare elites, and treasure chests across the expansion's zones. This is a recolor / lookalike variant — the items may share appearances with a base set from a different expansion, so they can drop in zones outside the listed expansion range.

### Farming Logistics
No lockout — farm continuously. Individual drop rates from any specific mob are low, so most collectors check the **Auction House** first (these pieces are bind-on-equip and often listed). Rare-elite hunts on Time-walking weeks or Cross-Realm group routes massively speed up the search.

### Farming Tips
Leather armor, available to Rogue, Druid, Monk, Demon Hunter. Install Handynotes plus the relevant expansion's "Rares + Treasures" plugin to map every spawn. AoE classes (Frost Mage, Havoc DH, Outlaw Rogue) clear contested zones faster than single-target builds.

455.content:
### How to Obtain
World-drop appearance from **Cataclysm** content. Pieces drop from outdoor mobs, rare elites, and treasure chests across the expansion's zones. This is a recolor / lookalike variant — the items may share appearances with a base set from a different expansion, so they can drop in zones outside the listed expansion range.

### Farming Logistics
No lockout — farm continuously. Individual drop rates from any specific mob are low, so most collectors check the **Auction House** first (these pieces are bind-on-equip and often listed). Rare-elite hunts on Time-walking weeks or Cross-Realm group routes massively speed up the search.

### Farming Tips
Wearable by any class — appearance is shared across all armor types. Install Handynotes plus the relevant expansion's "Rares + Treasures" plugin to map every spawn. AoE classes (Frost Mage, Havoc DH, Outlaw Rogue) clear contested zones faster than single-target builds.

Drops from bosses inside **a Classic-era raid** (Classic). Each main armor slot (Head, Shoulders, Chest, Hands, Legs, Feet, Waist, Wrists) drops from a specific boss in the raid — open the set on Wowhead via the link below for the exact boss-to-slot mapping.

### Farming Logistics
Standard raid weekly lockout — the full instance resets every Tuesday. This is the Normal difficulty version of the appearance. Other difficulties (LFR / Normal / Heroic / Mythic) use separate tints and are tracked as separate sets. At current max level the raid is usually soloable or trivially small-group, so you can collect missing pieces 1–2 weeks at a time.

### Farming Tips
Cloth armor, available to Mage, Priest, Warlock. Use the Great Vault and any bonus-roll currency from this expansion to maximize attempts per week. If a piece keeps not dropping, target-farm the specific boss and skip the rest.

496.content:
### How to Obtain
World-drop appearance from **Cataclysm** content. Pieces drop from outdoor mobs, rare elites, and treasure chests across the expansion's zones. This is a recolor / lookalike variant — the items may share appearances with a base set from a different expansion, so they can drop in zones outside the listed expansion range.

### Farming Logistics
No lockout — farm continuously. Individual drop rates from any specific mob are low, so most collectors check the **Auction House** first (these pieces are bind-on-equip and often listed). Rare-elite hunts on Time-walking weeks or Cross-Realm group routes massively speed up the search.

### Farming Tips
Wearable by any class — appearance is shared across all armor types. Install Handynotes plus the relevant expansion's "Rares + Treasures" plugin to map every spawn. AoE classes (Frost Mage, Havoc DH, Outlaw Rogue) clear contested zones faster than single-target builds.

### How to Obtain
Thassarian's Battlegear is a Cataclysm appearance set. The exact source isn't categorized in our database — open the Wowhead link below for the canonical piece list and drop locations.

### Farming Logistics
Wearable by any class — appearance is shared across all armor types. Most legacy sets at current max level are soloable; modern current-expansion sets may require active content engagement. Check the Wowhead set page for difficulty and source details.

If pieces are bind-on-equip, check the Auction House on your realm before farming directly. The Wardrobe automatically learns appearances on first loot pickup, so it's safe to roll Need even if you'll vendor the item later.

Runetotem's Battlegear is a Cataclysm appearance set. The exact source isn't categorized in our database — open the Wowhead link below for the canonical piece list and drop locations.

### Farming Logistics
Wearable by any class — appearance is shared across all armor types. Most legacy sets at current max level are soloable; modern current-expansion sets may require active content engagement. Check the Wowhead set page for difficulty and source details.

### Farming Tips
If pieces are bind-on-equip, check the Auction House on your realm before farming directly. The Wardrobe automatically learns appearances on first loot pickup, so it's safe to roll Need even if you'll vendor the item later.

522.content:
### How to Obtain
Drops from bosses inside **a Cataclysm-era raid** (Cataclysm). Each main armor slot (Head, Shoulders, Chest, Hands, Legs, Feet, Waist, Wrists) drops from a specific boss in the raid — open the set on Wowhead via the link below for the exact boss-to-slot mapping.

### Farming Logistics
Standard raid weekly lockout — the full instance resets every Tuesday. This is the Normal difficulty version of the appearance. Other difficulties (LFR / Normal / Heroic / Mythic) use separate tints and are tracked as separate sets. At current max level the raid is usually soloable or trivially small-group, so you can collect missing pieces 1–2 weeks at a time.

### Farming Tips
Wearable by any class — appearance is shared across all armor types. Use the Great Vault and any bonus-roll currency from this expansion to maximize attempts per week. If a piece keeps not dropping, target-farm the specific boss and skip the rest.

538.content:
### How to Obtain
World-drop appearance from **Cataclysm** content. Pieces drop from outdoor mobs, rare elites, and treasure chests across the expansion's zones. This is a recolor / lookalike variant — the items may share appearances with a base set from a different expansion, so they can drop in zones outside the listed expansion range.

### Farming Logistics
No lockout — farm continuously. Individual drop rates from any specific mob are low, so most collectors check the **Auction House** first (these pieces are bind-on-equip and often listed). Rare-elite hunts on Time-walking weeks or Cross-Realm group routes massively speed up the search.

### Farming Tips
Wearable by any class — appearance is shared across all armor types. Install Handynotes plus the relevant expansion's "Rares + Treasures" plugin to map every spawn. AoE classes (Frost Mage, Havoc DH, Outlaw Rogue) clear contested zones faster than single-target builds.

557.content:
### How to Obtain
PvP appearance from Wrath of the Lich King. Earned through Rated PvP (Arenas, Rated Battlegrounds, or Solo Shuffle) during the active season by spending **Arena Points and Honor** at the seasonal PvP quartermaster.

### Farming Logistics
PvP appearances are tied to the season they were released in. The base appearance is available at low rating tiers (Combatant / Rival, around 1400 rating). If the original season is over, the appearance is no longer purchasable through normal means — check the **Black Market Auction House** (currently in Valdrakken) which occasionally lists legacy PvP pieces.

### Farming Tips
Cloth armor, available to Mage, Priest, Warlock. If you collected the set during its active season it is permanently unlocked in your Wardrobe even if the vendor no longer sells it. Check your Wardrobe before grinding — appearances persist across characters on the same account.

This is a recolor / lookalike variant — the items may share appearances with a base set from a different expansion, so they can drop in zones outside the listed expansion range.

### Farming Logistics
No lockout — farm continuously. Individual drop rates from any specific mob are low, so most collectors check the **Auction House** first (these pieces are bind-on-equip and often listed). Rare-elite hunts on Time-walking weeks or Cross-Realm group routes massively speed up the search.

### Farming Tips
Wearable by any class — appearance is shared across all armor types. Install Handynotes plus the relevant expansion's "Rares + Treasures" plugin to map every spawn. AoE classes (Frost Mage, Havoc DH, Outlaw Rogue) clear contested zones faster than single-target builds.

566.content:
### How to Obtain
Drops from bosses inside **a Cataclysm-era raid** (Cataclysm). Each main armor slot (Head, Shoulders, Chest, Hands, Legs, Feet, Waist, Wrists) drops from a specific boss in the raid — open the set on Wowhead via the link below for the exact boss-to-slot mapping.

### Farming Logistics
Standard raid weekly lockout — the full instance resets every Tuesday. This is the Normal difficulty version of the appearance. Other difficulties (LFR / Normal / Heroic / Mythic) use separate tints and are tracked as separate sets. At current max level the raid is usually soloable or trivially small-group, so you can collect missing pieces 1–2 weeks at a time.

### Farming Tips
Wearable by any class — appearance is shared across all armor types. Use the Great Vault and any bonus-roll currency from this expansion to maximize attempts per week. If a piece keeps not dropping, target-farm the specific boss and skip the rest.

Vestments of Transcendence is a Burning Crusade appearance set. The exact source isn't categorized in our database — open the Wowhead link below for the canonical piece list and drop locations.

### Farming Logistics
Cloth armor, available to Mage, Priest, Warlock. Most legacy sets at current max level are soloable; modern current-expansion sets may require active content engagement. Check the Wowhead set page for difficulty and source details.

### Farming Tips
If pieces are bind-on-equip, check the Auction House on your realm before farming directly. The Wardrobe automatically learns appearances on first loot pickup, so it's safe to roll Need even if you'll vendor the item later.

604.content:
### How to Obtain
World-drop appearance from **Cataclysm** content. Pieces drop from outdoor mobs, rare elites, and treasure chests across the expansion's zones. This is a recolor / lookalike variant — the items may share appearances with a base set from a different expansion, so they can drop in zones outside the listed expansion range.

### Farming Logistics
No lockout — farm continuously. Individual drop rates from any specific mob are low, so most collectors check the **Auction House** first (these pieces are bind-on-equip and often listed). Rare-elite hunts on Time-walking weeks or Cross-Realm group routes massively speed up the search.

### Farming Tips
Wearable by any class — appearance is shared across all armor types. Install Handynotes plus the relevant expansion's "Rares + Treasures" plugin to map every spawn. AoE classes (Frost Mage, Havoc DH, Outlaw Rogue) clear contested zones faster than single-target builds.

611.content:
### How to Obtain
Drops from bosses inside **a Wrath of the Lich King-era raid** (Wrath of the Lich King). Each main armor slot (Head, Shoulders, Chest, Hands, Legs, Feet, Waist, Wrists) drops from a specific boss in the raid — open the set on Wowhead via the link below for the exact boss-to-slot mapping.

### Farming Logistics
Standard raid weekly lockout — the full instance resets every Tuesday. This is the Normal difficulty version of the appearance. Other difficulties (LFR / Normal / Heroic / Mythic) use separate tints and are tracked as separate sets. At current max level the raid is usually soloable or trivially small-group, so you can collect missing pieces 1–2 weeks at a time.

### Farming Tips
Wearable by any class — appearance is shared across all armor types. Use the Great Vault and any bonus-roll currency from this expansion to maximize attempts per week. If a piece keeps not dropping, target-farm the specific boss and skip the rest.

Drops from bosses inside **a Mists of Pandaria-era raid** (Mists of Pandaria). Each main armor slot (Head, Shoulders, Chest, Hands, Legs, Feet, Waist, Wrists) drops from a specific boss in the raid — open the set on Wowhead via the link below for the exact boss-to-slot mapping.

### Farming Logistics
Standard raid weekly lockout — the full instance resets every Tuesday. This is the Normal difficulty version of the appearance. Other difficulties (LFR / Normal / Heroic / Mythic) use separate tints and are tracked as separate sets. At current max level the raid is usually soloable or trivially small-group, so you can collect missing pieces 1–2 weeks at a time.

### Farming Tips
Wearable by any class — appearance is shared across all armor types. Use the Great Vault and any bonus-roll currency from this expansion to maximize attempts per week. If a piece keeps not dropping, target-farm the specific boss and skip the rest.

648.content:
### How to Obtain
World-drop appearance from **Cataclysm** content. Pieces drop from outdoor mobs, rare elites, and treasure chests across the expansion's zones. This is a recolor / lookalike variant — the items may share appearances with a base set from a different expansion, so they can drop in zones outside the listed expansion range.

### Farming Logistics
No lockout — farm continuously. Individual drop rates from any specific mob are low, so most collectors check the **Auction House** first (these pieces are bind-on-equip and often listed). Rare-elite hunts on Time-walking weeks or Cross-Realm group routes massively speed up the search.

### Farming Tips
Wearable by any class — appearance is shared across all armor types. Install Handynotes plus the relevant expansion's "Rares + Treasures" plugin to map every spawn. AoE classes (Frost Mage, Havoc DH, Outlaw Rogue) clear contested zones faster than single-target builds.

If pieces are bind-on-equip, check the Auction House on your realm before farming directly. The Wardrobe automatically learns appearances on first loot pickup, so it's safe to roll Need even if you'll vendor the item later.

692.content:
### How to Obtain
World-drop appearance from **Cataclysm** content. Pieces drop from outdoor mobs, rare elites, and treasure chests across the expansion's zones. This is a recolor / lookalike variant — the items may share appearances with a base set from a different expansion, so they can drop in zones outside the listed expansion range.

### Farming Logistics
No lockout — farm continuously. Individual drop rates from any specific mob are low, so most collectors check the **Auction House** first (these pieces are bind-on-equip and often listed). Rare-elite hunts on Time-walking weeks or Cross-Realm group routes massively speed up the search.

### Farming Tips
Wearable by any class — appearance is shared across all armor types. Install Handynotes plus the relevant expansion's "Rares + Treasures" plugin to map every spawn. AoE classes (Frost Mage, Havoc DH, Outlaw Rogue) clear contested zones faster than single-target builds.

700.content:
### How to Obtain
PvP appearance from Wrath of the Lich King. Earned through Rated PvP (Arenas, Rated Battlegrounds, or Solo Shuffle) during the active season by spending **Arena Points and Honor** at the seasonal PvP quartermaster.

### Farming Logistics
PvP appearances are tied to the season they were released in. The base appearance is available at low rating tiers (Combatant / Rival, around 1400 rating). If the original season is over, the appearance is no longer purchasable through normal means — check the **Black Market Auction House** (currently in Valdrakken) which occasionally lists legacy PvP pieces.

### Farming Tips
Wearable by any class — appearance is shared across all armor types. If you collected the set during its active season it is permanently unlocked in your Wardrobe even if the vendor no longer sells it. Check your Wardrobe before grinding — appearances persist across characters on the same account.

### Farming Logistics
Wearable by any class — appearance is shared across all armor types. Most legacy sets at current max level are soloable; modern current-expansion sets may require active content engagement. Check the Wowhead set page for difficulty and source details.

If pieces are bind-on-equip, check the Auction House on your realm before farming directly. The Wardrobe automatically learns appearances on first loot pickup, so it's safe to roll Need even if you'll vendor the item later.

PvP appearance from Wrath of the Lich King. Earned through Rated PvP (Arenas, Rated Battlegrounds, or Solo Shuffle) during the active season by spending **Arena Points and Honor** at the seasonal PvP quartermaster.

### Farming Logistics
PvP appearances are tied to the season they were released in. The base appearance is available at low rating tiers (Combatant / Rival, around 1400 rating). If the original season is over, the appearance is no longer purchasable through normal means — check the **Black Market Auction House** (currently in Valdrakken) which occasionally lists legacy PvP pieces.

### Farming Tips
Wearable by any class — appearance is shared across all armor types. If you collected the set during its active season it is permanently unlocked in your Wardrobe even if the vendor no longer sells it. Check your Wardrobe before grinding — appearances persist across characters on the same account.

929.content:
### How to Obtain
Drops from bosses inside **a The War Within-era raid** (The War Within). Each main armor slot (Head, Shoulders, Chest, Hands, Legs, Feet, Waist, Wrists) drops from a specific boss in the raid — open the set on Wowhead via the link below for the exact boss-to-slot mapping.

### Farming Logistics
Standard raid weekly lockout — the full instance resets every Tuesday. This is the Heroic difficulty version of the appearance. Other difficulties (LFR / Normal / Heroic / Mythic) use separate tints and are tracked as separate sets. At current max level the raid is usually soloable or trivially small-group, so you can collect missing pieces 1–2 weeks at a time.

### Farming Tips
Wearable by any class — appearance is shared across all armor types. Use the Great Vault and any bonus-roll currency from this expansion to maximize attempts per week. If a piece keeps not dropping, target-farm the specific boss and skip the rest.

936.content:
### How to Obtain
PvP appearance from Wrath of the Lich King. Earned through Rated PvP (Arenas, Rated Battlegrounds, or Solo Shuffle) during the active season by spending **Arena Points and Honor** at the seasonal PvP quartermaster.

### Farming Logistics
PvP appearances are tied to the season they were released in. The base appearance is available at low rating tiers (Combatant / Rival, around 1400 rating). If the original season is over, the appearance is no longer purchasable through normal means — check the **Black Market Auction House** (currently in Valdrakken) which occasionally lists legacy PvP pieces.

### Farming Tips
Wearable by any class — appearance is shared across all armor types. If you collected the set during its active season it is permanently unlocked in your Wardrobe even if the vendor no longer sells it. Check your Wardrobe before grinding — appearances persist across characters on the same account.

959.content:
### How to Obtain
World-drop appearance from **Wrath of the Lich King** content. Pieces drop from outdoor mobs, rare elites, and treasure chests across the expansion's zones. This is a recolor / lookalike variant — the items may share appearances with a base set from a different expansion, so they can drop in zones outside the listed expansion range.

### Farming Logistics
No lockout — farm continuously. Individual drop rates from any specific mob are low, so most collectors check the **Auction House** first (these pieces are bind-on-equip and often listed). Rare-elite hunts on Time-walking weeks or Cross-Realm group routes massively speed up the search.

### Farming Tips
Wearable by any class — appearance is shared across all armor types. Install Handynotes plus the relevant expansion's "Rares + Treasures" plugin to map every spawn. AoE classes (Frost Mage, Havoc DH, Outlaw Rogue) clear contested zones faster than single-target builds.

994.content:
### How to Obtain
World-drop appearance from **Wrath of the Lich King** content. Pieces drop from outdoor mobs, rare elites, and treasure chests across the expansion's zones. This is a recolor / lookalike variant — the items may share appearances with a base set from a different expansion, so they can drop in zones outside the listed expansion range.

### Farming Logistics
No lockout — farm continuously. Individual drop rates from any specific mob are low, so most collectors check the **Auction House** first (these pieces are bind-on-equip and often listed). Rare-elite hunts on Time-walking weeks or Cross-Realm group routes massively speed up the search.

### Farming Tips
Wearable by any class — appearance is shared across all armor types. Install Handynotes plus the relevant expansion's "Rares + Treasures" plugin to map every spawn. AoE classes (Frost Mage, Havoc DH, Outlaw Rogue) clear contested zones faster than single-target builds.

World-drop appearance from **Cataclysm** content. Pieces drop from outdoor mobs, rare elites, and treasure chests across the expansion's zones. This is a recolor / lookalike variant — the items may share appearances with a base set from a different expansion, so they can drop in zones outside the listed expansion range.

### Farming Logistics
No lockout — farm continuously. Individual drop rates from any specific mob are low, so most collectors check the **Auction House** first (these pieces are bind-on-equip and often listed). Rare-elite hunts on Time-walking weeks or Cross-Realm group routes massively speed up the search.

### Farming Tips
Cloth armor, available to Mage, Priest, Warlock. Install Handynotes plus the relevant expansion's "Rares + Treasures" plugin to map every spawn. AoE classes (Frost Mage, Havoc DH, Outlaw Rogue) clear contested zones faster than single-target builds.

1108.content:
### How to Obtain
Charscale Battlegear is a Cataclysm appearance set. The exact source isn't categorized in our database — open the Wowhead link below for the canonical piece list and drop locations.

### Farming Logistics
Wearable by any class — appearance is shared across all armor types. Most legacy sets at current max level are soloable; modern current-expansion sets may require active content engagement. Check the Wowhead set page for difficulty and source details.

### Farming Tips
If pieces are bind-on-equip, check the Auction House on your realm before farming directly. The Wardrobe automatically learns appearances on first loot pickup, so it's safe to roll Need even if you'll vendor the item later.

1117.content:
### How to Obtain
Drops from bosses inside **a Mists of Pandaria-era raid** (Mists of Pandaria). Each main armor slot (Head, Shoulders, Chest, Hands, Legs, Feet, Waist, Wrists) drops from a specific boss in the raid — open the set on Wowhead via the link below for the exact boss-to-slot mapping.

### Farming Logistics
Standard raid weekly lockout — the full instance resets every Tuesday. This is the Heroic difficulty version of the appearance. Other difficulties (LFR / Normal / Heroic / Mythic) use separate tints and are tracked as separate sets. At current max level the raid is usually soloable or trivially small-group, so you can collect missing pieces 1–2 weeks at a time.

### Farming Tips
Plate armor, available to Warrior, Paladin, Death Knight. Use the Great Vault and any bonus-roll currency from this expansion to maximize attempts per week. If a piece keeps not dropping, target-farm the specific boss and skip the rest.

1126.content:
### How to Obtain
Available from the **Trading Post**, located in Orgrimmar (Valley of Strength) or Stormwind (Trade District). Purchased with **Trader's Tender**, the currency earned by completing monthly Traveler's Log activities (about 500 Tender per month plus bonus activities).

### Farming Logistics
Trading Post inventory rotates **monthly** — if this set is not in the current month's catalog, it will eventually return in a future rotation. Tender carries over indefinitely between months, so you can save up for higher-priced items. No lockout once purchased.

### Farming Tips
Wearable by any class — appearance is shared across all armor types. Complete every Traveler's Log activity each month to maximize Tender — they cover all content (dungeons, raids, PvP, world quests, professions). Set bundles typically cost 500–750 Tender; individual pieces cost less.

1181.content:
### How to Obtain
Drops from bosses inside **a Burning Crusade-era raid** (Burning Crusade). Each main armor slot (Head, Shoulders, Chest, Hands, Legs, Feet, Waist, Wrists) drops from a specific boss in the raid — open the set on Wowhead via the link below for the exact boss-to-slot mapping.

### Farming Logistics
Standard raid weekly lockout — the full instance resets every Tuesday. This is the Heroic difficulty version of the appearance. Other difficulties (LFR / Normal / Heroic / Mythic) use separate tints and are tracked as separate sets. At current max level the raid is usually soloable or trivially small-group, so you can collect missing pieces 1–2 weeks at a time.

### Farming Tips
Wearable by any class — appearance is shared across all armor types. Use the Great Vault and any bonus-roll currency from this expansion to maximize attempts per week. If a piece keeps not dropping, target-farm the specific boss and skip the rest.

1224.content:
### How to Obtain
PvP appearance from Cataclysm. Earned through Rated PvP (Arenas, Rated Battlegrounds, or Solo Shuffle) during the active season by spending **Conquest Points and Honor** at the seasonal PvP quartermaster.

### Farming Logistics
PvP appearances are tied to the season they were released in. The base appearance is available at low rating tiers (Combatant / Rival, around 1400 rating). If the original season is over, the appearance is no longer purchasable through normal means — check the **Black Market Auction House** (currently in Valdrakken) which occasionally lists legacy PvP pieces.

### Farming Tips
Wearable by any class — appearance is shared across all armor types. If you collected the set during its active season it is permanently unlocked in your Wardrobe even if the vendor no longer sells it. Check your Wardrobe before grinding — appearances persist across characters on the same account.

1225.content:
### How to Obtain
PvP appearance from Cataclysm. Earned through Rated PvP (Arenas, Rated Battlegrounds, or Solo Shuffle) during the active season by spending **Conquest Points and Honor** at the seasonal PvP quartermaster.

### Farming Logistics
PvP appearances are tied to the season they were released in. This is the Elite recolor variant — it requires a higher Rated PvP threshold (typically Duelist 2100+ or Gladiator 2400+) to purchase, earned during the original season. If the original season is over, the appearance is no longer purchasable through normal means — check the **Black Market Auction House** (currently in Valdrakken) which occasionally lists legacy PvP pieces.

### Farming Tips
Leather armor, available to Rogue, Druid, Monk, Demon Hunter. If you collected the set during its active season it is permanently unlocked in your Wardrobe even if the vendor no longer sells it. Check your Wardrobe before grinding — appearances persist across characters on the same account.

1244.content:
### How to Obtain
Drops from bosses inside **a Mists of Pandaria-era raid** (Mists of Pandaria). Each main armor slot (Head, Shoulders, Chest, Hands, Legs, Feet, Waist, Wrists) drops from a specific boss in the raid — open the set on Wowhead via the link below for the exact boss-to-slot mapping.

### Farming Logistics
Standard raid weekly lockout — the full instance resets every Tuesday. This is the Normal difficulty version of the appearance. Other difficulties (LFR / Normal / Heroic / Mythic) use separate tints and are tracked as separate sets. At current max level the raid is usually soloable or trivially small-group, so you can collect missing pieces 1–2 weeks at a time.

### Farming Tips
Wearable by any class — appearance is shared across all armor types. Use the Great Vault and any bonus-roll currency from this expansion to maximize attempts per week. If a piece keeps not dropping, target-farm the specific boss and skip the rest.

1247.content:
### How to Obtain
Sorrow Plate is a Mists of Pandaria appearance set. The exact source isn't categorized in our database — open the Wowhead link below for the canonical piece list and drop locations.

### Farming Logistics
Wearable by any class — appearance is shared across all armor types. Most legacy sets at current max level are soloable; modern current-expansion sets may require active content engagement. Check the Wowhead set page for difficulty and source details.

### Farming Tips
If pieces are bind-on-equip, check the Auction House on your realm before farming directly. The Wardrobe automatically learns appearances on first loot pickup, so it's safe to roll Need even if you'll vendor the item later.

1248.content:
### How to Obtain
World-drop appearance from **Mists of Pandaria** content. Pieces drop from outdoor mobs, rare elites, and treasure chests across the expansion's zones. This is a recolor / lookalike variant — the items may share appearances with a base set from a different expansion, so they can drop in zones outside the listed expansion range.

### Farming Logistics
No lockout — farm continuously. Individual drop rates from any specific mob are low, so most collectors check the **Auction House** first (these pieces are bind-on-equip and often listed). Rare-elite hunts on Time-walking weeks or Cross-Realm group routes massively speed up the search.

### Farming Tips
Plate armor, available to Warrior, Paladin, Death Knight. Install Handynotes plus the relevant expansion's "Rares + Treasures" plugin to map every spawn. AoE classes (Frost Mage, Havoc DH, Outlaw Rogue) clear contested zones faster than single-target builds.

Plate armor, available to Warrior, Paladin, Death Knight. Most legacy sets at current max level are soloable; modern current-expansion sets may require active content engagement. Check the Wowhead set page for difficulty and source details.

If pieces are bind-on-equip, check the Auction House on your realm before farming directly. The Wardrobe automatically learns appearances on first loot pickup, so it's safe to roll Need even if you'll vendor the item later.

1348.content:
### How to Obtain
Drops from bosses inside **Naxxramas** (Wrath of the Lich King). Each main armor slot (Head, Shoulders, Chest, Hands, Legs, Feet, Waist, Wrists) drops from a specific boss in the raid — open the set on Wowhead via the link below for the exact boss-to-slot mapping.

### Farming Logistics
Standard raid weekly lockout — the full instance resets every Tuesday. Raid bosses have a chance to drop set pieces on each clear; difficulties (LFR / Normal / Heroic / Mythic) share the same items but render with different color tints. At current max level the raid is usually soloable or trivially small-group, so you can collect missing pieces 1–2 weeks at a time.

### Farming Tips
Wearable by any class — appearance is shared across all armor types. Use the Great Vault and any bonus-roll currency from this expansion to maximize attempts per week. If a piece keeps not dropping, target-farm the specific boss and skip the rest.

1387.content:
### How to Obtain
PvP appearance from Mists of Pandaria. Earned through Rated PvP (Arenas, Rated Battlegrounds, or Solo Shuffle) during the active season by spending **Conquest Points and Honor** at the seasonal PvP quartermaster.

### Farming Logistics
PvP appearances are tied to the season they were released in. The base appearance is available at low rating tiers (Combatant / Rival, around 1400 rating). If the original season is over, the appearance is no longer purchasable through normal means — check the **Black Market Auction House** (currently in Valdrakken) which occasionally lists legacy PvP pieces.

### Farming Tips
Wearable by any class — appearance is shared across all armor types. If you collected the set during its active season it is permanently unlocked in your Wardrobe even if the vendor no longer sells it. Check your Wardrobe before grinding — appearances persist across characters on the same account.

PvP appearance from Mists of Pandaria. Earned through Rated PvP (Arenas, Rated Battlegrounds, or Solo Shuffle) during the active season by spending **Conquest Points and Honor** at the seasonal PvP quartermaster.

### Farming Logistics
PvP appearances are tied to the season they were released in. The base appearance is available at low rating tiers (Combatant / Rival, around 1400 rating). If the original season is over, the appearance is no longer purchasable through normal means — check the **Black Market Auction House** (currently in Valdrakken) which occasionally lists legacy PvP pieces.

### Farming Tips
Wearable by any class — appearance is shared across all armor types. If you collected the set during its active season it is permanently unlocked in your Wardrobe even if the vendor no longer sells it. Check your Wardrobe before grinding — appearances persist across characters on the same account.

1419.content:
### How to Obtain
Drops from bosses inside **a Warlords of Draenor-era raid** (Warlords of Draenor). Each main armor slot (Head, Shoulders, Chest, Hands, Legs, Feet, Waist, Wrists) drops from a specific boss in the raid — open the set on Wowhead via the link below for the exact boss-to-slot mapping.

### Farming Logistics
Standard raid weekly lockout — the full instance resets every Tuesday. This is the Heroic difficulty version of the appearance. Other difficulties (LFR / Normal / Heroic / Mythic) use separate tints and are tracked as separate sets. At current max level the raid is usually soloable or trivially small-group, so you can collect missing pieces 1–2 weeks at a time.

### Farming Tips
Cloth armor, available to Mage, Priest, Warlock. Use the Great Vault and any bonus-roll currency from this expansion to maximize attempts per week. If a piece keeps not dropping, target-farm the specific boss and skip the rest.

1471.content:
### How to Obtain
Eternal Dynasty Regalia is a Warlords of Draenor appearance set. The exact source isn't categorized in our database — open the Wowhead link below for the canonical piece list and drop locations.

### Farming Logistics
Wearable by any class — appearance is shared across all armor types. Most legacy sets at current max level are soloable; modern current-expansion sets may require active content engagement. Check the Wowhead set page for difficulty and source details.

### Farming Tips
If pieces are bind-on-equip, check the Auction House on your realm before farming directly. The Wardrobe automatically learns appearances on first loot pickup, so it's safe to roll Need even if you'll vendor the item later.

1472.content:
### How to Obtain
World-drop appearance from **Warlords of Draenor** content. Pieces drop from outdoor mobs, rare elites, and treasure chests across the expansion's zones. This is a recolor / lookalike variant — the items may share appearances with a base set from a different expansion, so they can drop in zones outside the listed expansion range.

### Farming Logistics
No lockout — farm continuously. Individual drop rates from any specific mob are low, so most collectors check the **Auction House** first (these pieces are bind-on-equip and often listed). Rare-elite hunts on Time-walking weeks or Cross-Realm group routes massively speed up the search.

### Farming Tips
Wearable by any class — appearance is shared across all armor types. Install Handynotes plus the relevant expansion's "Rares + Treasures" plugin to map every spawn. AoE classes (Frost Mage, Havoc DH, Outlaw Rogue) clear contested zones faster than single-target builds.

Lich Lord Plate is a Warlords of Draenor appearance set. The exact source isn't categorized in our database — open the Wowhead link below for the canonical piece list and drop locations.

### Farming Logistics
Wearable by any class — appearance is shared across all armor types. Most legacy sets at current max level are soloable; modern current-expansion sets may require active content engagement. Check the Wowhead set page for difficulty and source details.

### Farming Tips
If pieces are bind-on-equip, check the Auction House on your realm before farming directly. The Wardrobe automatically learns appearances on first loot pickup, so it's safe to roll Need even if you'll vendor the item later.

1532.content:
### How to Obtain
Drops from bosses inside **a Warlords of Draenor-era raid** (Warlords of Draenor). Each main armor slot (Head, Shoulders, Chest, Hands, Legs, Feet, Waist, Wrists) drops from a specific boss in the raid — open the set on Wowhead via the link below for the exact boss-to-slot mapping.

### Farming Logistics
Standard raid weekly lockout — the full instance resets every Tuesday. This is the Heroic difficulty version of the appearance. Other difficulties (LFR / Normal / Heroic / Mythic) use separate tints and are tracked as separate sets. At current max level the raid is usually soloable or trivially small-group, so you can collect missing pieces 1–2 weeks at a time.

### Farming Tips
Wearable by any class — appearance is shared across all armor types. Use the Great Vault and any bonus-roll currency from this expansion to maximize attempts per week. If a piece keeps not dropping, target-farm the specific boss and skip the rest.

1533.content:
### How to Obtain
PvP appearance from Warlords of Draenor. Earned through Rated PvP (Arenas, Rated Battlegrounds, or Solo Shuffle) during the active season by spending **Conquest and Honor** at the seasonal PvP quartermaster.

### Farming Logistics
PvP appearances are tied to the season they were released in. The base appearance is available at low rating tiers (Combatant / Rival, around 1400 rating). If the original season is over, the appearance is no longer purchasable through normal means — check the **Black Market Auction House** (currently in Valdrakken) which occasionally lists legacy PvP pieces.

### Farming Tips
Wearable by any class — appearance is shared across all armor types. If you collected the set during its active season it is permanently unlocked in your Wardrobe even if the vendor no longer sells it. Check your Wardrobe before grinding — appearances persist across characters on the same account.

1536.content:
### How to Obtain
World-drop appearance from **Cataclysm** content. Pieces drop from outdoor mobs, rare elites, and treasure chests across the expansion's zones. This is a recolor / lookalike variant — the items may share appearances with a base set from a different expansion, so they can drop in zones outside the listed expansion range.

### Farming Logistics
No lockout — farm continuously. Individual drop rates from any specific mob are low, so most collectors check the **Auction House** first (these pieces are bind-on-equip and often listed). Rare-elite hunts on Time-walking weeks or Cross-Realm group routes massively speed up the search.

### Farming Tips
Wearable by any class — appearance is shared across all armor types. Install Handynotes plus the relevant expansion's "Rares + Treasures" plugin to map every spawn. AoE classes (Frost Mage, Havoc DH, Outlaw Rogue) clear contested zones faster than single-target builds.

Wearable by any class — appearance is shared across all armor types. If you collected the set during its active season it is permanently unlocked in your Wardrobe even if the vendor no longer sells it. Check your Wardrobe before grinding — appearances persist across characters on the same account.

1581.content:
### How to Obtain
Drops from bosses inside **a Warlords of Draenor-era raid** (Warlords of Draenor). Each main armor slot (Head, Shoulders, Chest, Hands, Legs, Feet, Waist, Wrists) drops from a specific boss in the raid — open the set on Wowhead via the link below for the exact boss-to-slot mapping.

### Farming Logistics
Standard raid weekly lockout — the full instance resets every Tuesday. This is the Normal difficulty version of the appearance. Other difficulties (LFR / Normal / Heroic / Mythic) use separate tints and are tracked as separate sets. At current max level the raid is usually soloable or trivially small-group, so you can collect missing pieces 1–2 weeks at a time.

### Farming Tips
Wearable by any class — appearance is shared across all armor types. Use the Great Vault and any bonus-roll currency from this expansion to maximize attempts per week. If a piece keeps not dropping, target-farm the specific boss and skip the rest.

Drops from bosses inside **a Warlords of Draenor-era raid** (Warlords of Draenor). Each main armor slot (Head, Shoulders, Chest, Hands, Legs, Feet, Waist, Wrists) drops from a specific boss in the raid — open the set on Wowhead via the link below for the exact boss-to-slot mapping.

### Farming Logistics
Standard raid weekly lockout — the full instance resets every Tuesday. This is the Heroic difficulty version of the appearance. Other difficulties (LFR / Normal / Heroic / Mythic) use separate tints and are tracked as separate sets. At current max level the raid is usually soloable or trivially small-group, so you can collect missing pieces 1–2 weeks at a time.

### Farming Tips
Wearable by any class — appearance is shared across all armor types. Use the Great Vault and any bonus-roll currency from this expansion to maximize attempts per week. If a piece keeps not dropping, target-farm the specific boss and skip the rest.

1647.content:
### How to Obtain
World-drop appearance from **Wrath of the Lich King** content. Pieces drop from outdoor mobs, rare elites, and treasure chests across the expansion's zones. This is a recolor / lookalike variant — the items may share appearances with a base set from a different expansion, so they can drop in zones outside the listed expansion range.

### Farming Logistics
No lockout — farm continuously. Individual drop rates from any specific mob are low, so most collectors check the **Auction House** first (these pieces are bind-on-equip and often listed). Rare-elite hunts on Time-walking weeks or Cross-Realm group routes massively speed up the search.

### Farming Tips
Wearable by any class — appearance is shared across all armor types. Install Handynotes plus the relevant expansion's "Rares + Treasures" plugin to map every spawn. AoE classes (Frost Mage, Havoc DH, Outlaw Rogue) clear contested zones faster than single-target builds.

1662.content:
### How to Obtain
Crimson Mail Set is a Classic appearance set. The exact source isn't categorized in our database — open the Wowhead link below for the canonical piece list and drop locations.

### Farming Logistics
Wearable by any class — appearance is shared across all armor types. Most legacy sets at current max level are soloable; modern current-expansion sets may require active content engagement. Check the Wowhead set page for difficulty and source details.

### Farming Tips
If pieces are bind-on-equip, check the Auction House on your realm before farming directly. The Wardrobe automatically learns appearances on first loot pickup, so it's safe to roll Need even if you'll vendor the item later.

### Farming Logistics
Wearable by any class — appearance is shared across all armor types. Most legacy sets at current max level are soloable; modern current-expansion sets may require active content engagement. Check the Wowhead set page for difficulty and source details.

### Farming Tips
If pieces are bind-on-equip, check the Auction House on your realm before farming directly. The Wardrobe automatically learns appearances on first loot pickup, so it's safe to roll Need even if you'll vendor the item later.

1664.content:
### How to Obtain
World-drop appearance from **Cataclysm** content. Pieces drop from outdoor mobs, rare elites, and treasure chests across the expansion's zones. This is a recolor / lookalike variant — the items may share appearances with a base set from a different expansion, so they can drop in zones outside the listed expansion range.

### Farming Logistics
No lockout — farm continuously. Individual drop rates from any specific mob are low, so most collectors check the **Auction House** first (these pieces are bind-on-equip and often listed). Rare-elite hunts on Time-walking weeks or Cross-Realm group routes massively speed up the search.

### Farming Tips
Wearable by any class — appearance is shared across all armor types. Install Handynotes plus the relevant expansion's "Rares + Treasures" plugin to map every spawn. AoE classes (Frost Mage, Havoc DH, Outlaw Rogue) clear contested zones faster than single-target builds.

1666.content:
### How to Obtain
Drops from bosses inside **a Mists of Pandaria-era raid** (Mists of Pandaria). Each main armor slot (Head, Shoulders, Chest, Hands, Legs, Feet, Waist, Wrists) drops from a specific boss in the raid — open the set on Wowhead via the link below for the exact boss-to-slot mapping.

### Farming Logistics
Standard raid weekly lockout — the full instance resets every Tuesday. This is the Heroic difficulty version of the appearance. Other difficulties (LFR / Normal / Heroic / Mythic) use separate tints and are tracked as separate sets. At current max level the raid is usually soloable or trivially small-group, so you can collect missing pieces 1–2 weeks at a time.

### Farming Tips
Wearable by any class — appearance is shared across all armor types. Use the Great Vault and any bonus-roll currency from this expansion to maximize attempts per week. If a piece keeps not dropping, target-farm the specific boss and skip the rest.

1672.content:
### How to Obtain
PvP appearance from Mists of Pandaria. Earned through Rated PvP (Arenas, Rated Battlegrounds, or Solo Shuffle) during the active season by spending **Conquest Points and Honor** at the seasonal PvP quartermaster.

### Farming Logistics
PvP appearances are tied to the season they were released in. The base appearance is available at low rating tiers (Combatant / Rival, around 1400 rating). If the original season is over, the appearance is no longer purchasable through normal means — check the **Black Market Auction House** (currently in Valdrakken) which occasionally lists legacy PvP pieces.

### Farming Tips
Wearable by any class — appearance is shared across all armor types. If you collected the set during its active season it is permanently unlocked in your Wardrobe even if the vendor no longer sells it. Check your Wardrobe before grinding — appearances persist across characters on the same account.

If the original season is over, the appearance is no longer purchasable through normal means — check the **Black Market Auction House** (currently in Valdrakken) which occasionally lists legacy PvP pieces.

### Farming Tips
Plate armor, available to Warrior, Paladin, Death Knight. If you collected the set during its active season it is permanently unlocked in your Wardrobe even if the vendor no longer sells it. Check your Wardrobe before grinding — appearances persist across characters on the same account.

1706.content:
### How to Obtain
Available from the **Trading Post**, located in Orgrimmar (Valley of Strength) or Stormwind (Trade District). Purchased with **Trader's Tender**, the currency earned by completing monthly Traveler's Log activities (about 500 Tender per month plus bonus activities).

### Farming Logistics
Trading Post inventory rotates **monthly** — if this set is not in the current month's catalog, it will eventually return in a future rotation. Tender carries over indefinitely between months, so you can save up for higher-priced items. No lockout once purchased.

### Farming Tips
Cloth armor, available to Mage, Priest, Warlock. Complete every Traveler's Log activity each month to maximize Tender — they cover all content (dungeons, raids, PvP, world quests, professions). Set bundles typically cost 500–750 Tender; individual pieces cost less.

1708.content:
### How to Obtain
Drops from bosses inside **a Burning Crusade-era raid** (Burning Crusade). Each main armor slot (Head, Shoulders, Chest, Hands, Legs, Feet, Waist, Wrists) drops from a specific boss in the raid — open the set on Wowhead via the link below for the exact boss-to-slot mapping.

### Farming Logistics
Standard raid weekly lockout — the full instance resets every Tuesday. This is the Heroic difficulty version of the appearance. Other difficulties (LFR / Normal / Heroic / Mythic) use separate tints and are tracked as separate sets. At current max level the raid is usually soloable or trivially small-group, so you can collect missing pieces 1–2 weeks at a time.

### Farming Tips
Wearable by any class — appearance is shared across all armor types. Use the Great Vault and any bonus-roll currency from this expansion to maximize attempts per week. If a piece keeps not dropping, target-farm the specific boss and skip the rest.

1711.content:
### How to Obtain
World-drop appearance from **Wrath of the Lich King** content. Pieces drop from outdoor mobs, rare elites, and treasure chests across the expansion's zones. This is a recolor / lookalike variant — the items may share appearances with a base set from a different expansion, so they can drop in zones outside the listed expansion range.

### Farming Logistics
No lockout — farm continuously. Individual drop rates from any specific mob are low, so most collectors check the **Auction House** first (these pieces are bind-on-equip and often listed). Rare-elite hunts on Time-walking weeks or Cross-Realm group routes massively speed up the search.

### Farming Tips
Cloth armor, available to Mage, Priest, Warlock. Install Handynotes plus the relevant expansion's "Rares + Treasures" plugin to map every spawn. AoE classes (Frost Mage, Havoc DH, Outlaw Rogue) clear contested zones faster than single-target builds.

Cloth armor, available to Mage, Priest, Warlock. Use the Great Vault and any bonus-roll currency from this expansion to maximize attempts per week. If a piece keeps not dropping, target-farm the specific boss and skip the rest.

1790.content:
### How to Obtain
Kor'kron Dark Shaman Armor is a Mists of Pandaria appearance set. The exact source isn't categorized in our database — open the Wowhead link below for the canonical piece list and drop locations.

### Farming Logistics
Mail armor, available to Shaman. Most legacy sets at current max level are soloable; modern current-expansion sets may require active content engagement. Check the Wowhead set page for difficulty and source details.

### Farming Tips
If pieces are bind-on-equip, check the Auction House on your realm before farming directly. The Wardrobe automatically learns appearances on first loot pickup, so it's safe to roll Need even if you'll vendor the item later.

1791.content:
### How to Obtain
World-drop appearance from **Classic** content. Pieces drop from outdoor mobs, rare elites, and treasure chests across the expansion's zones. This is a recolor / lookalike variant — the items may share appearances with a base set from a different expansion, so they can drop in zones outside the listed expansion range.

### Farming Logistics
No lockout — farm continuously. Individual drop rates from any specific mob are low, so most collectors check the **Auction House** first (these pieces are bind-on-equip and often listed). Rare-elite hunts on Time-walking weeks or Cross-Realm group routes massively speed up the search.

### Farming Tips
Plate armor, available to Warrior, Paladin, Death Knight. Install Handynotes plus the relevant expansion's "Rares + Treasures" plugin to map every spawn. AoE classes (Frost Mage, Havoc DH, Outlaw Rogue) clear contested zones faster than single-target builds.

Orgrimmar Set is a Legion appearance set. The exact source isn't categorized in our database — open the Wowhead link below for the canonical piece list and drop locations.

### Farming Logistics
Wearable by any class — appearance is shared across all armor types. Most legacy sets at current max level are soloable; modern current-expansion sets may require active content engagement. Check the Wowhead set page for difficulty and source details.

### Farming Tips
If pieces are bind-on-equip, check the Auction House on your realm before farming directly. The Wardrobe automatically learns appearances on first loot pickup, so it's safe to roll Need even if you'll vendor the item later.

1802.content:
### How to Obtain
PvP appearance from Legion. Earned through Rated PvP (Arenas, Rated Battlegrounds, or Solo Shuffle) during the active season by spending **Marks of Honor and seasonal Conquest** at the seasonal PvP quartermaster.

### Farming Logistics
PvP appearances are tied to the season they were released in. The base appearance is available at low rating tiers (Combatant / Rival, around 1400 rating). If the original season is over, the appearance is no longer purchasable through normal means — check the **Black Market Auction House** (currently in Valdrakken) which occasionally lists legacy PvP pieces.

### Farming Tips
Cloth armor, available to Mage, Priest, Warlock. If you collected the set during its active season it is permanently unlocked in your Wardrobe even if the vendor no longer sells it. Check your Wardrobe before grinding — appearances persist across characters on the same account.

1835.content:
### How to Obtain
Drops from bosses inside **a Legion-era raid** (Legion). Each main armor slot (Head, Shoulders, Chest, Hands, Legs, Feet, Waist, Wrists) drops from a specific boss in the raid — open the set on Wowhead via the link below for the exact boss-to-slot mapping.

### Farming Logistics
Standard raid weekly lockout — the full instance resets every Tuesday. This is the Normal difficulty version of the appearance. Other difficulties (LFR / Normal / Heroic / Mythic) use separate tints and are tracked as separate sets. Drops from bosses inside **a Legion-era raid** (Legion). Each main armor slot (Head, Shoulders, Chest, Hands, Legs, Feet, Waist, Wrists) drops from a specific boss in the raid — open the set on Wowhead via the link below for the exact boss-to-slot mapping.

### Farming Logistics
Standard raid weekly lockout — the full instance resets every Tuesday. This is the Heroic difficulty version of the appearance. Other difficulties (LFR / Normal / Heroic / Mythic) use separate tints and are tracked as separate sets. At current max level the raid is usually soloable or trivially small-group, so you can collect missing pieces 1–2 weeks at a time.

### Farming Tips
Wearable by any class — appearance is shared across all armor types. Use the Great Vault and any bonus-roll currency from this expansion to maximize attempts per week. If a piece keeps not dropping, target-farm the specific boss and skip the rest.

1901.content:
### How to Obtain
World-drop appearance from **Legion** content. Pieces drop from outdoor mobs, rare elites, and treasure chests across the expansion's zones. This is a recolor / lookalike variant — the items may share appearances with a base set from a different expansion, so they can drop in zones outside the listed expansion range.

### Farming Logistics
No lockout — farm continuously. Individual drop rates from any specific mob are low, so most collectors check the **Auction House** first (these pieces are bind-on-equip and often listed). Rare-elite hunts on Time-walking weeks or Cross-Realm group routes massively speed up the search.

### Farming Tips
Cloth armor, available to Mage, Priest, Warlock. Install Handynotes plus the relevant expansion's "Rares + Treasures" plugin to map every spawn. AoE classes (Frost Mage, Havoc DH, Outlaw Rogue) clear contested zones faster than single-target builds.

1913.content:
### How to Obtain
PvP appearance from Legion. Earned through Rated PvP (Arenas, Rated Battlegrounds, or Solo Shuffle) during the active season by spending **Marks of Honor and seasonal Conquest** at the seasonal PvP quartermaster.

### Farming Logistics
PvP appearances are tied to the season they were released in. The base appearance is available at low rating tiers (Combatant / Rival, around 1400 rating). If the original season is over, the appearance is no longer purchasable through normal means — check the **Black Market Auction House** (currently in Valdrakken) which occasionally lists legacy PvP pieces.

### Farming Tips
Cloth armor, available to Mage, Priest, Warlock. If you collected the set during its active season it is permanently unlocked in your Wardrobe even if the vendor no longer sells it. Check your Wardrobe before grinding — appearances persist across characters on the same account.

This is a recolor / lookalike variant — the items may share appearances with a base set from a different expansion, so they can drop in zones outside the listed expansion range.

### Farming Logistics
No lockout — farm continuously. Individual drop rates from any specific mob are low, so most collectors check the **Auction House** first (these pieces are bind-on-equip and often listed). Rare-elite hunts on Time-walking weeks or Cross-Realm group routes massively speed up the search.

### Farming Tips
Wearable by any class — appearance is shared across all armor types. Install Handynotes plus the relevant expansion's "Rares + Treasures" plugin to map every spawn. AoE classes (Frost Mage, Havoc DH, Outlaw Rogue) clear contested zones faster than single-target builds.

1984.content:
### How to Obtain
Drops from bosses inside **a Legion-era raid** (Legion). Each main armor slot (Head, Shoulders, Chest, Hands, Legs, Feet, Waist, Wrists) drops from a specific boss in the raid — open the set on Wowhead via the link below for the exact boss-to-slot mapping.

### Farming Logistics
Standard raid weekly lockout — the full instance resets every Tuesday. This is the Normal difficulty version of the appearance. Other difficulties (LFR / Normal / Heroic / Mythic) use separate tints and are tracked as separate sets. At current max level the raid is usually soloable or trivially small-group, so you can collect missing pieces 1–2 weeks at a time.

### Farming Tips
Wearable by any class — appearance is shared across all armor types. Use the Great Vault and any bonus-roll currency from this expansion to maximize attempts per week. If a piece keeps not dropping, target-farm the specific boss and skip the rest.

Drops from bosses inside **a Legion-era raid** (Legion). Each main armor slot (Head, Shoulders, Chest, Hands, Legs, Feet, Waist, Wrists) drops from a specific boss in the raid — open the set on Wowhead via the link below for the exact boss-to-slot mapping.

### Farming Logistics
Standard raid weekly lockout — the full instance resets every Tuesday. This is the Heroic difficulty version of the appearance. Other difficulties (LFR / Normal / Heroic / Mythic) use separate tints and are tracked as separate sets. At current max level the raid is usually soloable or trivially small-group, so you can collect missing pieces 1–2 weeks at a time.

### Farming Tips
Cloth armor, available to Mage, Priest, Warlock. Use the Great Vault and any bonus-roll currency from this expansion to maximize attempts per week. If a piece keeps not dropping, target-farm the specific boss and skip the rest.

At current max level the raid is usually soloable or trivially small-group, so you can collect missing pieces 1–2 weeks at a time.

### Farming Tips
Wearable by any class — appearance is shared across all armor types. Use the Great Vault and any bonus-roll currency from this expansion to maximize attempts per week. If a piece keeps not dropping, target-farm the specific boss and skip the rest.

2049.content:
### How to Obtain
Drops from bosses inside **a Legion-era raid** (Legion). Each main armor slot (Head, Shoulders, Chest, Hands, Legs, Feet, Waist, Wrists) drops from a specific boss in the raid — open the set on Wowhead via the link below for the exact boss-to-slot mapping.

### Farming Logistics
Standard raid weekly lockout — the full instance resets every Tuesday. This is the Mythic difficulty version of the appearance. Other difficulties (LFR / Normal / Heroic / Mythic) use separate tints and are tracked as separate sets. At current max level the raid is usually soloable or trivially small-group, so you can collect missing pieces 1–2 weeks at a time.

2050.content:
### How to Obtain
PvP appearance from Legion. Earned through Rated PvP (Arenas, Rated Battlegrounds, or Solo Shuffle) during the active season by spending **Marks of Honor and seasonal Conquest** at the seasonal PvP quartermaster.

### Farming Logistics
PvP appearances are tied to the season they were released in. The base appearance is available at low rating tiers (Combatant / Rival, around 1400 rating). If the original season is over, the appearance is no longer purchasable through normal means — check the **Black Market Auction House** (currently in Valdrakken) which occasionally lists legacy PvP pieces.

### Farming Tips
Wearable by any class — appearance is shared across all armor types. If you collected the set during its active season it is permanently unlocked in your Wardrobe even if the vendor no longer sells it. Check your Wardrobe before grinding — appearances persist across characters on the same account.

If the original season is over, the appearance is no longer purchasable through normal means — check the **Black Market Auction House** (currently in Valdrakken) which occasionally lists legacy PvP pieces.

### Farming Tips
Wearable by any class — appearance is shared across all armor types. If you collected the set during its active season it is permanently unlocked in your Wardrobe even if the vendor no longer sells it. Check your Wardrobe before grinding — appearances persist across characters on the same account.

2083.content:
### How to Obtain
World-drop appearance from **Legion** content. Pieces drop from outdoor mobs, rare elites, and treasure chests across the expansion's zones. This is a recolor / lookalike variant — the items may share appearances with a base set from a different expansion, so they can drop in zones outside the listed expansion range.

### Farming Logistics
No lockout — farm continuously. Individual drop rates from any specific mob are low, so most collectors check the **Auction House** first (these pieces are bind-on-equip and often listed). Rare-elite hunts on Time-walking weeks or Cross-Realm group routes massively speed up the search.

### Farming Tips
Wearable by any class — appearance is shared across all armor types. Install Handynotes plus the relevant expansion's "Rares + Treasures" plugin to map every spawn. AoE classes (Frost Mage, Havoc DH, Outlaw Rogue) clear contested zones faster than single-target builds.

2092.content:
### How to Obtain
PvP appearance from Legion. Earned through Rated PvP (Arenas, Rated Battlegrounds, or Solo Shuffle) during the active season by spending **Marks of Honor and seasonal Conquest** at the seasonal PvP quartermaster.

PvP appearances are tied to the season they were released in. The base appearance is available at low rating tiers (Combatant / Rival, around 1400 rating). Battlelord's Plate is a Battle for Azeroth appearance set. The exact source isn't categorized in our database — open the Wowhead link below for the canonical piece list and drop locations.

### Farming Logistics
Wearable by any class — appearance is shared across all armor types. Most legacy sets at current max level are soloable; modern current-expansion sets may require active content engagement. Check the Wowhead set page for difficulty and source details.

### Farming Tips
If pieces are bind-on-equip, check the Auction House on your realm before farming directly. The Wardrobe automatically learns appearances on first loot pickup, so it's safe to roll Need even if you'll vendor the item later.

2127.content:
### How to Obtain
PvP appearance from Legion. Earned through Rated PvP (Arenas, Rated Battlegrounds, or Solo Shuffle) during the active season by spending **Marks of Honor and seasonal Conquest** at the seasonal PvP quartermaster.

### Farming Logistics
PvP appearances are tied to the season they were released in. The base appearance is available at low rating tiers (Combatant / Rival, around 1400 rating). If the original season is over, the appearance is no longer purchasable through normal means — check the **Black Market Auction House** (currently in Valdrakken) which occasionally lists legacy PvP pieces.

### Farming Tips
Wearable by any class — appearance is shared across all armor types. If you collected the set during its active season it is permanently unlocked in your Wardrobe even if the vendor no longer sells it. Check your Wardrobe before grinding — appearances persist across characters on the same account.

2174.content:
### How to Obtain
Regalia of Everburning Knowledge is a Battle for Azeroth appearance set. The exact source isn't categorized in our database — open the Wowhead link below for the canonical piece list and drop locations.

### Farming Logistics
Wearable by any class — appearance is shared across all armor types. Most legacy sets at current max level are soloable; modern current-expansion sets may require active content engagement. Check the Wowhead set page for difficulty and source details.

### Farming Tips
If pieces are bind-on-equip, check the Auction House on your realm before farming directly. The Wardrobe automatically learns appearances on first loot pickup, so it's safe to roll Need even if you'll vendor the item later.

2175.content:
### How to Obtain
Drops from bosses inside **a Battle for Azeroth-era raid** (Battle for Azeroth). Each main armor slot (Head, Shoulders, Chest, Hands, Legs, Feet, Waist, Wrists) drops from a specific boss in the raid — open the set on Wowhead via the link below for the exact boss-to-slot mapping.

### Farming Logistics
Standard raid weekly lockout — the full instance resets every Tuesday. This is the Heroic difficulty version of the appearance. Other difficulties (LFR / Normal / Heroic / Mythic) use separate tints and are tracked as separate sets. At current max level the raid is usually soloable or trivially small-group, so you can collect missing pieces 1–2 weeks at a time.

### Farming Tips
Wearable by any class — appearance is shared across all armor types. Use the Great Vault and any bonus-roll currency from this expansion to maximize attempts per week. If a piece keeps not dropping, target-farm the specific boss and skip the rest.

Drops from bosses inside **a Battle for Azeroth-era raid** (Battle for Azeroth). Each main armor slot (Head, Shoulders, Chest, Hands, Legs, Feet, Waist, Wrists) drops from a specific boss in the raid — open the set on Wowhead via the link below for the exact boss-to-slot mapping.

### Farming Logistics
Standard raid weekly lockout — the full instance resets every Tuesday. This is the Normal difficulty version of the appearance. Other difficulties (LFR / Normal / Heroic / Mythic) use separate tints and are tracked as separate sets. At current max level the raid is usually soloable or trivially small-group, so you can collect missing pieces 1–2 weeks at a time.

### Farming Tips
Wearable by any class — appearance is shared across all armor types. Use the Great Vault and any bonus-roll currency from this expansion to maximize attempts per week. If a piece keeps not dropping, target-farm the specific boss and skip the rest.
