2271.content:
### How to Obtain
Legion Cloth Quest Armor is a Legion appearance set. The exact source isn't categorized in our database — open the Wowhead link below for the canonical piece list and drop locations.

### Farming Logistics
Wearable by any class — appearance is shared across all armor types. Most legacy sets at current max level are soloable; modern current-expansion sets may require active content engagement. Check the Wowhead set page for difficulty and source details.

### Farming Tips
If pieces are bind-on-equip, check the Auction House on your realm before farming directly. The Wardrobe automatically learns appearances on first loot pickup, so it's safe to roll Need even if you'll vendor the item later.

2272.content:
### How to Obtain
World-drop appearance from **Legion** content. Pieces drop from outdoor mobs, rare elites, and treasure chests across the expansion's zones. This is a recolor / lookalike variant — the items may share appearances with a base set from a different expansion, so they can drop in zones outside the listed expansion range.

### Farming Logistics
No lockout — farm continuously. Individual drop rates from any specific mob are low, so most collectors check the **Auction House** first (these pieces are bind-on-equip and often listed). Rare-elite hunts on Time-walking weeks or Cross-Realm group routes massively speed up the search.

### Farming Tips
Wearable by any class — appearance is shared across all armor types. Install Handynotes plus the relevant expansion's "Rares + Treasures" plugin to map every spawn. AoE classes (Frost Mage, Havoc DH, Outlaw Rogue) clear contested zones faster than single-target builds.

2299.content:
### How to Obtain
Drops from dungeon bosses in Legion-era dungeons. Set pieces are spread across multiple dungeons in the expansion — open the Wowhead link below to see the exact dungeon and boss for each slot.

### Farming Logistics
Dungeons have a per-character daily reset rather than a weekly lockout, so you can rerun the same dungeon repeatedly for missing pieces. At current max level the content is fully soloable in 5–10 minutes per run. Drop rates per relevant boss are typically 15–25 %.

### Farming Tips
Plate armor, available to Warrior, Paladin, Death Knight. Mythic+ does not change the appearance — running on Normal is the fastest way to farm. Pick a class with strong AoE for speed; alts with the same armor type can funnel duplicate drops.

Drops from bosses inside **a Legion-era raid** (Legion). Each main armor slot (Head, Shoulders, Chest, Hands, Legs, Feet, Waist, Wrists) drops from a specific boss in the raid — open the set on Wowhead via the link below for the exact boss-to-slot mapping.

### Farming Logistics
Standard raid weekly lockout — the full instance resets every Tuesday. This is the Heroic difficulty version of the appearance. Other difficulties (LFR / Normal / Heroic / Mythic) use separate tints and are tracked as separate sets. At current max level the raid is usually soloable or trivially small-group, so you can collect missing pieces 1–2 weeks at a time.

### Farming Tips
Plate armor, available to Warrior, Paladin, Death Knight. Use the Great Vault and any bonus-roll currency from this expansion to maximize attempts per week. If a piece keeps not dropping, target-farm the specific boss and skip the rest.

2307.content:
### How to Obtain
Vestments of the Chosen Dead is a Battle for Azeroth appearance set. The exact source isn't categorized in our database — open the Wowhead link below for the canonical piece list and drop locations.

### Farming Logistics
Wearable by any class — appearance is shared across all armor types. Most legacy sets at current max level are soloable; modern current-expansion sets may require active content engagement. Check the Wowhead set page for difficulty and source details.

### Farming Tips
If pieces are bind-on-equip, check the Auction House on your realm before farming directly. The Wardrobe automatically learns appearances on first loot pickup, so it's safe to roll Need even if you'll vendor the item later.

2339.content:
### How to Obtain
PvP appearance from Legion. Earned through Rated PvP (Arenas, Rated Battlegrounds, or Solo Shuffle) during the active season by spending **Marks of Honor and seasonal Conquest** at the seasonal PvP quartermaster.

### Farming Logistics
PvP appearances are tied to the season they were released in. The base appearance is available at low rating tiers (Combatant / Rival, around 1400 rating). If the original season is over, the appearance is no longer purchasable through normal means — check the **Black Market Auction House** (currently in Valdrakken) which occasionally lists legacy PvP pieces.

### Farming Tips
Wearable by any class — appearance is shared across all armor types. If you collected the set during its active season it is permanently unlocked in your Wardrobe even if the vendor no longer sells it. Check your Wardrobe before grinding — appearances persist across characters on the same account.

2375.content:
### How to Obtain
Regalia of the Arcane Tempest is a Battle for Azeroth appearance set. The exact source isn't categorized in our database — open the Wowhead link below for the canonical piece list and drop locations.

### Farming Logistics
Wearable by any class — appearance is shared across all armor types. Most legacy sets at current max level are soloable; modern current-expansion sets may require active content engagement. Check the Wowhead set page for difficulty and source details.

### Farming Tips
If pieces are bind-on-equip, check the Auction House on your realm before farming directly. The Wardrobe automatically learns appearances on first loot pickup, so it's safe to roll Need even if you'll vendor the item later.

2376.content:
### How to Obtain
Drops from bosses inside **a Battle for Azeroth-era raid** (Battle for Azeroth). Each main armor slot (Head, Shoulders, Chest, Hands, Legs, Feet, Waist, Wrists) drops from a specific boss in the raid — open the set on Wowhead via the link below for the exact boss-to-slot mapping.

### Farming Logistics
Standard raid weekly lockout — the full instance resets every Tuesday. This is the Heroic difficulty version of the appearance. Other difficulties (LFR / Normal / Heroic / Mythic) use separate tints and are tracked as separate sets. At current max level the raid is usually soloable or trivially small-group, so you can collect missing pieces 1–2 weeks at a time.

### Farming Tips
Wearable by any class — appearance is shared across all armor types. Use the Great Vault and any bonus-roll currency from this expansion to maximize attempts per week. If a piece keeps not dropping, target-farm the specific boss and skip the rest.

2471.content:
### How to Obtain
PvP appearance from Legion. Earned through Rated PvP (Arenas, Rated Battlegrounds, or Solo Shuffle) during the active season by spending **Marks of Honor and seasonal Conquest** at the seasonal PvP quartermaster.

### Farming Logistics
PvP appearances are tied to the season they were released in. The base appearance is available at low rating tiers (Combatant / Rival, around 1400 rating). If the original season is over, the appearance is no longer purchasable through normal means — check the **Black Market Auction House** (currently in Valdrakken) which occasionally lists legacy PvP pieces.

### Farming Tips
Wearable by any class — appearance is shared across all armor types. If you collected the set during its active season it is permanently unlocked in your Wardrobe even if the vendor no longer sells it. Check your Wardrobe before grinding — appearances persist across characters on the same account.

PvP appearance from Mists of Pandaria. Earned through Rated PvP (Arenas, Rated Battlegrounds, or Solo Shuffle) during the active season by spending **Conquest Points and Honor** at the seasonal PvP quartermaster.

### Farming Logistics
PvP appearances are tied to the season they were released in. The base appearance is available at low rating tiers (Combatant / Rival, around 1400 rating). If the original season is over, the appearance is no longer purchasable through normal means — check the **Black Market Auction House** (currently in Valdrakken) which occasionally lists legacy PvP pieces.

### Farming Tips
Plate armor, available to Warrior, Paladin, Death Knight. If you collected the set during its active season it is permanently unlocked in your Wardrobe even if the vendor no longer sells it. Check your Wardrobe before grinding — appearances persist across characters on the same account.

2523.content:
### How to Obtain
World-drop appearance from **Battle for Azeroth** content. Pieces drop from outdoor mobs, rare elites, and treasure chests across the expansion's zones. This is a recolor / lookalike variant — the items may share appearances with a base set from a different expansion, so they can drop in zones outside the listed expansion range.

### Farming Logistics
No lockout — farm continuously. Individual drop rates from any specific mob are low, so most collectors check the **Auction House** first (these pieces are bind-on-equip and often listed). Rare-elite hunts on Time-walking weeks or Cross-Realm group routes massively speed up the search.

### Farming Tips
Wearable by any class — appearance is shared across all armor types. Install Handynotes plus the relevant expansion's "Rares + Treasures" plugin to map every spawn. AoE classes (Frost Mage, Havoc DH, Outlaw Rogue) clear contested zones faster than single-target builds.

2525.content:
### How to Obtain
Broken Shore Leather is a Battle for Azeroth appearance set. The exact source isn't categorized in our database — open the Wowhead link below for the canonical piece list and drop locations.

### Farming Logistics
Wearable by any class — appearance is shared across all armor types. Most legacy sets at current max level are soloable; modern current-expansion sets may require active content engagement. Check the Wowhead set page for difficulty and source details.

### Farming Tips
If pieces are bind-on-equip, check the Auction House on your realm before farming directly. The Wardrobe automatically learns appearances on first loot pickup, so it's safe to roll Need even if you'll vendor the item later.

2571.content:
### How to Obtain
World-drop appearance from **Warlords of Draenor** content. Pieces drop from outdoor mobs, rare elites, and treasure chests across the expansion's zones. This is a recolor / lookalike variant — the items may share appearances with a base set from a different expansion, so they can drop in zones outside the listed expansion range.

### Farming Logistics
No lockout — farm continuously. Individual drop rates from any specific mob are low, so most collectors check the **Auction House** first (these pieces are bind-on-equip and often listed). Rare-elite hunts on Time-walking weeks or Cross-Realm group routes massively speed up the search.

### Farming Tips
Wearable by any class — appearance is shared across all armor types. Install Handynotes plus the relevant expansion's "Rares + Treasures" plugin to map every spawn. AoE classes (Frost Mage, Havoc DH, Outlaw Rogue) clear contested zones faster than single-target builds.

This is the Elite recolor variant — it requires a higher Rated PvP threshold (typically Duelist 2100+ or Gladiator 2400+) to purchase, earned during the original season. If the original season is over, the appearance is no longer purchasable through normal means — check the **Black Market Auction House** (currently in Valdrakken) which occasionally lists legacy PvP pieces.

### Farming Tips
Leather armor, available to Rogue, Druid, Monk, Demon Hunter. If you collected the set during its active season it is permanently unlocked in your Wardrobe even if the vendor no longer sells it. Check your Wardrobe before grinding — appearances persist across characters on the same account.

2644.content:
### How to Obtain
Runebound Regalia is a Battle for Azeroth appearance set. The exact source isn't categorized in our database — open the Wowhead link below for the canonical piece list and drop locations.

### Farming Logistics
Wearable by any class — appearance is shared across all armor types. Most legacy sets at current max level are soloable; modern current-expansion sets may require active content engagement. Check the Wowhead set page for difficulty and source details.

### Farming Tips
If pieces are bind-on-equip, check the Auction House on your realm before farming directly. The Wardrobe automatically learns appearances on first loot pickup, so it's safe to roll Need even if you'll vendor the item later.

2645.content:
### How to Obtain
Drops from bosses inside **a Battle for Azeroth-era raid** (Battle for Azeroth). Each main armor slot (Head, Shoulders, Chest, Hands, Legs, Feet, Waist, Wrists) drops from a specific boss in the raid — open the set on Wowhead via the link below for the exact boss-to-slot mapping.

### Farming Logistics
Standard raid weekly lockout — the full instance resets every Tuesday. This is the Heroic difficulty version of the appearance. Other difficulties (LFR / Normal / Heroic / Mythic) use separate tints and are tracked as separate sets. At current max level the raid is usually soloable or trivially small-group, so you can collect missing pieces 1–2 weeks at a time.

### Farming Tips
Wearable by any class — appearance is shared across all armor types. Use the Great Vault and any bonus-roll currency from this expansion to maximize attempts per week. If a piece keeps not dropping, target-farm the specific boss and skip the rest.

2740.content:
### How to Obtain
World-drop appearance from **Battle for Azeroth** content. Pieces drop from outdoor mobs, rare elites, and treasure chests across the expansion's zones. This is a recolor / lookalike variant — the items may share appearances with a base set from a different expansion, so they can drop in zones outside the listed expansion range.

### Farming Logistics
No lockout — farm continuously. Individual drop rates from any specific mob are low, so most collectors check the **Auction House** first (these pieces are bind-on-equip and often listed). Rare-elite hunts on Time-walking weeks or Cross-Realm group routes massively speed up the search.

### Farming Tips
Wearable by any class — appearance is shared across all armor types. Install Handynotes plus the relevant expansion's "Rares + Treasures" plugin to map every spawn. AoE classes (Frost Mage, Havoc DH, Outlaw Rogue) clear contested zones faster than single-target builds.

2750.content:
### How to Obtain
Drops from dungeon bosses in Battle for Azeroth-era dungeons. Set pieces are spread across multiple dungeons in the expansion — open the Wowhead link below to see the exact dungeon and boss for each slot.

### Farming Logistics
Dungeons have a per-character daily reset rather than a weekly lockout, so you can rerun the same dungeon repeatedly for missing pieces. At current max level the content is fully soloable in 5–10 minutes per run. Drop rates per relevant boss are typically 15–25 %.

### Farming Tips
Wearable by any class — appearance is shared across all armor types. Mythic+ does not change the appearance — running on Normal is the fastest way to farm. Pick a class with strong AoE for speed; alts with the same armor type can funnel duplicate drops.

2759.content:
### How to Obtain
PvP appearance from Battle for Azeroth. Earned through Rated PvP (Arenas, Rated Battlegrounds, or Solo Shuffle) during the active season by spending **Conquest and Marks of Honor** at the seasonal PvP quartermaster.

### Farming Logistics
PvP appearances are tied to the season they were released in. The base appearance is available at low rating tiers (Combatant / Rival, around 1400 rating). If the original season is over, the appearance is no longer purchasable through normal means — check the **Black Market Auction House** (currently in Valdrakken) which occasionally lists legacy PvP pieces.

### Farming Tips
Wearable by any class — appearance is shared across all armor types. If you collected the set during its active season it is permanently unlocked in your Wardrobe even if the vendor no longer sells it. Check your Wardrobe before grinding — appearances persist across characters on the same account.

2761.content:
### How to Obtain
**Battle for Azeroth** Warfront cosmetic. Earned through Warfront content (Battle for Stromgarde or Battle for Darkshore) — pieces drop from the final Warfront boss and from quest reward caches when Alliance controls the contested zone.

### Farming Logistics
Warfronts run on a rotating schedule between Alliance and Horde control, with each cycle lasting about a week. You can only queue when your faction is in the attacking phase. Pieces are eligible from any Warfront completion during the active phase.

### Farming Tips
Wearable by any class — appearance is shared across all armor types. Check the Warfront calendar on Wowhead before logging in — running during an inactive phase wastes time. The Arathi Highlands and Darkshore Warfronts each have their own loot pool.

2765.content:
### How to Obtain
**Dark Iron Heritage Armor** — earned by leveling a Dark Iron character to max level and completing the Dark Iron Heritage Armor questline, which unlocks from the racial leader once you hit cap.

### Farming Logistics
One-time per Dark Iron character. The questline is short (15–30 minutes) but requires the level cap, so the real time investment is leveling. Heritage Armor cannot be earned via Boosts and requires a "from-scratch" leveling experience on some races.

### Farming Tips
Mail armor, available to Hunter, Shaman, Evoker. Heritage Armor is locked to the Dark Iron race specifically. The appearance is account-wide once unlocked — you can transmog it on any character of any class that race can play.

World-drop appearance from **Battle for Azeroth** content. Pieces drop from outdoor mobs, rare elites, and treasure chests across the expansion's zones. This is a recolor / lookalike variant — the items may share appearances with a base set from a different expansion, so they can drop in zones outside the listed expansion range.

### Farming Logistics
No lockout — farm continuously. Individual drop rates from any specific mob are low, so most collectors check the **Auction House** first (these pieces are bind-on-equip and often listed). Rare-elite hunts on Time-walking weeks or Cross-Realm group routes massively speed up the search.

### Farming Tips
Wearable by any class — appearance is shared across all armor types. Install Handynotes plus the relevant expansion's "Rares + Treasures" plugin to map every spawn. AoE classes (Frost Mage, Havoc DH, Outlaw Rogue) clear contested zones faster than single-target builds.

2789.content:
### How to Obtain
Drops from dungeon bosses in Battle for Azeroth-era dungeons. Set pieces are spread across multiple dungeons in the expansion — open the Wowhead link below to see the exact dungeon and boss for each slot.

### Farming Logistics
Dungeons have a per-character daily reset rather than a weekly lockout, so you can rerun the same dungeon repeatedly for missing pieces. At current max level the content is fully soloable in 5–10 minutes per run. Drop rates per relevant boss are typically 15–25 %.

### Farming Tips
Cloth armor, available to Mage, Priest, Warlock. Mythic+ does not change the appearance — running on Normal is the fastest way to farm. Pick a class with strong AoE for speed; alts with the same armor type can funnel duplicate drops.

2793.content:
### How to Obtain
Drops from bosses inside **a Battle for Azeroth-era raid** (Battle for Azeroth). Each main armor slot (Head, Shoulders, Chest, Hands, Legs, Feet, Waist, Wrists) drops from a specific boss in the raid — open the set on Wowhead via the link below for the exact boss-to-slot mapping.

### Farming Logistics
Standard raid weekly lockout — the full instance resets every Tuesday. This is the Normal difficulty version of the appearance. Other difficulties (LFR / Normal / Heroic / Mythic) use separate tints and are tracked as separate sets. At current max level the raid is usually soloable or trivially small-group, so you can collect missing pieces 1–2 weeks at a time.

### Farming Tips
Wearable by any class — appearance is shared across all armor types. Use the Great Vault and any bonus-roll currency from this expansion to maximize attempts per week. If a piece keeps not dropping, target-farm the specific boss and skip the rest.

2801.content:
### How to Obtain
PvP appearance from Battle for Azeroth. Earned through Rated PvP (Arenas, Rated Battlegrounds, or Solo Shuffle) during the active season by spending **Conquest and Marks of Honor** at the seasonal PvP quartermaster.

### Farming Logistics
PvP appearances are tied to the season they were released in. The base appearance is available at low rating tiers (Combatant / Rival, around 1400 rating). If the original season is over, the appearance is no longer purchasable through normal means — check the **Black Market Auction House** (currently in Valdrakken) which occasionally lists legacy PvP pieces.

### Farming Tips
Wearable by any class — appearance is shared across all armor types. If you collected the set during its active season it is permanently unlocked in your Wardrobe even if the vendor no longer sells it. Check your Wardrobe before grinding — appearances persist across characters on the same account.

2803.content:
### How to Obtain
**Battle for Azeroth** Warfront cosmetic. Earned through Warfront content (Battle for Stromgarde or Battle for Darkshore) — pieces drop from the final Warfront boss and from quest reward caches when Alliance controls the contested zone.

### Farming Logistics
Warfronts run on a rotating schedule between Alliance and Horde control, with each cycle lasting about a week. You can only queue when your faction is in the attacking phase. Pieces are eligible from any Warfront completion during the active phase.

### Farming Tips
Wearable by any class — appearance is shared across all armor types. Check the Warfront calendar on Wowhead before logging in — running during an inactive phase wastes time. The Arathi Highlands and Darkshore Warfronts each have their own loot pool.

Standard raid weekly lockout — the full instance resets every Tuesday. Raid bosses have a chance to drop set pieces on each clear; difficulties (LFR / Normal / Heroic / Mythic) share the same items but render with different color tints. At current max level the raid is usually soloable or trivially small-group, so you can collect missing pieces 1–2 weeks at a time.

### Farming Tips
Wearable by any class — appearance is shared across all armor types. Use the Great Vault and any bonus-roll currency from this expansion to maximize attempts per week. If a piece keeps not dropping, target-farm the specific boss and skip the rest.

2825.content:
### How to Obtain
PvP appearance from Battle for Azeroth. Earned through Rated PvP (Arenas, Rated Battlegrounds, or Solo Shuffle) during the active season by spending **Conquest and Marks of Honor** at the seasonal PvP quartermaster.

### Farming Logistics
PvP appearances are tied to the season they were released in. The base appearance is available at low rating tiers (Combatant / Rival, around 1400 rating). If the original season is over, the appearance is no longer purchasable through normal means — check the **Black Market Auction House** (currently in Valdrakken) which occasionally lists legacy PvP pieces.

### Farming Tips
Wearable by any class — appearance is shared across all armor types. If you collected the set during its active season it is permanently unlocked in your Wardrobe even if the vendor no longer sells it. Check your Wardrobe before grinding — appearances persist across characters on the same account.

2827.content:
### How to Obtain
**Battle for Azeroth** Warfront cosmetic. Earned through Warfront content (Battle for Stromgarde or Battle for Darkshore) — pieces drop from the final Warfront boss and from quest reward caches when Alliance controls the contested zone.

### Farming Logistics
Warfronts run on a rotating schedule between Alliance and Horde control, with each cycle lasting about a week. You can only queue when your faction is in the attacking phase. Pieces are eligible from any Warfront completion during the active phase.

### Farming Tips
Wearable by any class — appearance is shared across all armor types. Check the Warfront calendar on Wowhead before logging in — running during an inactive phase wastes time. The Arathi Highlands and Darkshore Warfronts each have their own loot pool.

2831.content:
### How to Obtain
World-drop appearance from **Battle for Azeroth** content. Pieces drop from outdoor mobs, rare elites, and treasure chests across the expansion's zones. This is a recolor / lookalike variant — the items may share appearances with a base set from a different expansion, so they can drop in zones outside the listed expansion range.

### Farming Logistics
No lockout — farm continuously. Individual drop rates from any specific mob are low, so most collectors check the **Auction House** first (these pieces are bind-on-equip and often listed). Rare-elite hunts on Time-walking weeks or Cross-Realm group routes massively speed up the search.

### Farming Tips
Wearable by any class — appearance is shared across all armor types. Install Handynotes plus the relevant expansion's "Rares + Treasures" plugin to map every spawn. AoE classes (Frost Mage, Havoc DH, Outlaw Rogue) clear contested zones faster than single-target builds.

2840.content:
### How to Obtain
Drops from dungeon bosses in Battle for Azeroth-era dungeons. Set pieces are spread across multiple dungeons in the expansion — open the Wowhead link below to see the exact dungeon and boss for each slot.

### Farming Logistics
Dungeons have a per-character daily reset rather than a weekly lockout, so you can rerun the same dungeon repeatedly for missing pieces. At current max level the content is fully soloable in 5–10 minutes per run. Drop rates per relevant boss are typically 15–25 %.

### Farming Tips
Wearable by any class — appearance is shared across all armor types. Mythic+ does not change the appearance — running on Normal is the fastest way to farm. Pick a class with strong AoE for speed; alts with the same armor type can funnel duplicate drops.

This is a recolor / lookalike variant — the items may share appearances with a base set from a different expansion, so they can drop in zones outside the listed expansion range.

### Farming Logistics
No lockout — farm continuously. Individual drop rates from any specific mob are low, so most collectors check the **Auction House** first (these pieces are bind-on-equip and often listed). Rare-elite hunts on Time-walking weeks or Cross-Realm group routes massively speed up the search.

### Farming Tips
Cloth armor, available to Warlock. Install Handynotes plus the relevant expansion's "Rares + Treasures" plugin to map every spawn. AoE classes (Frost Mage, Havoc DH, Outlaw Rogue) clear contested zones faster than single-target builds.

2871.content:
### How to Obtain
PvP appearance from Battle for Azeroth. Earned through Rated PvP (Arenas, Rated Battlegrounds, or Solo Shuffle) during the active season by spending **Conquest and Marks of Honor** at the seasonal PvP quartermaster.

### Farming Logistics
PvP appearances are tied to the season they were released in. The base appearance is available at low rating tiers (Combatant / Rival, around 1400 rating). If the original season is over, the appearance is no longer purchasable through normal means — check the **Black Market Auction House** (currently in Valdrakken) which occasionally lists legacy PvP pieces.

### Farming Tips
Wearable by any class — appearance is shared across all armor types. If you collected the set during its active season it is permanently unlocked in your Wardrobe even if the vendor no longer sells it. Check your Wardrobe before grinding — appearances persist across characters on the same account.

2873.content:
### How to Obtain
**Battle for Azeroth** Warfront cosmetic. Earned through Warfront content (Battle for Stromgarde or Battle for Darkshore) — pieces drop from the final Warfront boss and from quest reward caches when Horde controls the contested zone.

### Farming Logistics
Warfronts run on a rotating schedule between Alliance and Horde control, with each cycle lasting about a week. You can only queue when your faction is in the attacking phase. Pieces are eligible from any Warfront completion during the active phase.

### Farming Tips
Leather armor, available to Rogue, Druid, Monk, Demon Hunter. Check the Warfront calendar on Wowhead before logging in — running during an inactive phase wastes time. The Arathi Highlands and Darkshore Warfronts each have their own loot pool.

2896.content:
### How to Obtain
**Blood Elf Heritage Armor** — earned by leveling a Blood Elf character to max level and completing the Blood Elf Heritage Armor questline, which unlocks from the racial leader once you hit cap.

### Farming Logistics
One-time per Blood Elf character. The questline is short (15–30 minutes) but requires the level cap, so the real time investment is leveling. Heritage Armor cannot be earned via Boosts and requires a "from-scratch" leveling experience on some races.

### Farming Tips
Plate armor, available to Warrior, Paladin, Death Knight. Heritage Armor is locked to the Blood Elf race specifically. The appearance is account-wide once unlocked — you can transmog it on any character of any class that race can play.

2897.content:
### How to Obtain
**Dwarf Heritage Armor** — earned by leveling a Dwarf character to max level and completing the Dwarf Heritage Armor questline, which unlocks from the racial leader once you hit cap.

### Farming Logistics
One-time per Dwarf character. The questline is short (15–30 minutes) but requires the level cap, so the real time investment is leveling. Heritage Armor cannot be earned via Boosts and requires a "from-scratch" leveling experience on some races.

### Farming Tips
Wearable by any class — appearance is shared across all armor types. Heritage Armor is locked to the Dwarf race specifically. The appearance is account-wide once unlocked — you can transmog it on any character of any class that race can play.

2934.content:
### How to Obtain
Drops from bosses inside **a Shadowlands-era raid** (Shadowlands). Each main armor slot (Head, Shoulders, Chest, Hands, Legs, Feet, Waist, Wrists) drops from a specific boss in the raid — open the set on Wowhead via the link below for the exact boss-to-slot mapping.

### Farming Logistics
Standard raid weekly lockout — the full instance resets every Tuesday. This is the Normal difficulty version of the appearance. Other difficulties (LFR / Normal / Heroic / Mythic) use separate tints and are tracked as separate sets. At current max level the raid is usually soloable or trivially small-group, so you can collect missing pieces 1–2 weeks at a time.

### Farming Tips
Plate armor, available to Warrior, Paladin, Death Knight. Use the Great Vault and any bonus-roll currency from this expansion to maximize attempts per week. If a piece keeps not dropping, target-farm the specific boss and skip the rest.

2950.content:
### How to Obtain
**Kul Tiran Heritage Armor** — earned by leveling a Kul Tiran character to max level and completing the Kul Tiran Heritage Armor questline, which unlocks from the racial leader once you hit cap.

### Farming Logistics
One-time per Kul Tiran character. The questline is short (15–30 minutes) but requires the level cap, so the real time investment is leveling. Heritage Armor cannot be earned via Boosts and requires a "from-scratch" leveling experience on some races.

### Farming Tips
Wearable by any class — appearance is shared across all armor types. Heritage Armor is locked to the Kul Tiran race specifically. The appearance is account-wide once unlocked — you can transmog it on any character of any class that race can play.

2951.content:
### How to Obtain
**Zandalari Heritage Armor** — earned by leveling a Zandalari character to max level and completing the Zandalari Heritage Armor questline, which unlocks from the racial leader once you hit cap.

### Farming Logistics
One-time per Zandalari character. The questline is short (15–30 minutes) but requires the level cap, so the real time investment is leveling. Heritage Armor cannot be earned via Boosts and requires a "from-scratch" leveling experience on some races.

### Farming Tips
Mail armor, available to Hunter, Shaman, Evoker. Heritage Armor is locked to the Zandalari race specifically. The appearance is account-wide once unlocked — you can transmog it on any character of any class that race can play.

2955.content:
### How to Obtain
PvP appearance from Shadowlands. Earned through Rated PvP (Arenas, Rated Battlegrounds, or Solo Shuffle) during the active season by spending **Conquest, purchased from Zo'sorg in Oribos** at the seasonal PvP quartermaster.

### Farming Logistics
PvP appearances are tied to the season they were released in. The base appearance is available at low rating tiers (Combatant / Rival, around 1400 rating). If the original season is over, the appearance is no longer purchasable through normal means — check the **Black Market Auction House** (currently in Valdrakken) which occasionally lists legacy PvP pieces.

### Farming Tips
Wearable by any class — appearance is shared across all armor types. If you collected the set during its active season it is permanently unlocked in your Wardrobe even if the vendor no longer sells it. Check your Wardrobe before grinding — appearances persist across characters on the same account.

2979.content:
### How to Obtain
Drops from bosses inside **Eternal Palace** (Battle for Azeroth). Each main armor slot (Head, Shoulders, Chest, Hands, Legs, Feet, Waist, Wrists) drops from a specific boss in the raid — open the set on Wowhead via the link below for the exact boss-to-slot mapping.

### Farming Logistics
Standard raid weekly lockout — the full instance resets every Tuesday. This is the Normal difficulty version of the appearance. Other difficulties (LFR / Normal / Heroic / Mythic) use separate tints and are tracked as separate sets. At current max level the raid is usually soloable or trivially small-group, so you can collect missing pieces 1–2 weeks at a time.

### Farming Tips
Wearable by any class — appearance is shared across all armor types. Use the Great Vault and any bonus-roll currency from this expansion to maximize attempts per week. If a piece keeps not dropping, target-farm the specific boss and skip the rest.

2995.content:
### How to Obtain
**Gnome Heritage Armor** — earned by leveling a Gnome character to max level and completing the Gnome Heritage Armor questline, which unlocks from the racial leader once you hit cap.

### Farming Logistics
One-time per Gnome character. The questline is short (15–30 minutes) but requires the level cap, so the real time investment is leveling. Heritage Armor cannot be earned via Boosts and requires a "from-scratch" leveling experience on some races.

### Farming Tips
Cloth armor, available to Mage, Priest, Warlock. Heritage Armor is locked to the Gnome race specifically. The appearance is account-wide once unlocked — you can transmog it on any character of any class that race can play.

11831.content:
### How to Obtain
**Kyrian Covenant** cosmetic set from Shadowlands, earned in **Bastion**. Pieces unlock as part of the covenant campaign through Renown levels — pledge to Kyrian and progress the campaign chapters.

### Farming Logistics
Renown progresses via Covenant Callings (3 world quests per day in your covenant's zone), the campaign chapter quests, and Anima-spending activities. Returning players benefit from catch-up — full Renown can be earned in a few sessions rather than weeks.

### Farming Tips
Wearable by any class — appearance is shared across all armor types. You must be pledged to Kyrian specifically; the set is not available cross-covenant. The Bastion zone is also the source of mounts, pets, and other Kyrian-themed cosmetics worth picking up at the same time.

**Night Fae Covenant** cosmetic set from Shadowlands, earned in **Ardenweald**. Pieces unlock as part of the covenant campaign through Renown levels — pledge to Night Fae and progress the campaign chapters.

### Farming Logistics
Renown progresses via Covenant Callings (3 world quests per day in your covenant's zone), the campaign chapter quests, and Anima-spending activities. Returning players benefit from catch-up — full Renown can be earned in a few sessions rather than weeks.

### Farming Tips
Wearable by any class — appearance is shared across all armor types. You must be pledged to Night Fae specifically; the set is not available cross-covenant. The Ardenweald zone is also the source of mounts, pets, and other Night Fae-themed cosmetics worth picking up at the same time.

11907.content:
### How to Obtain
PvP appearance from Shadowlands. Earned through Rated PvP (Arenas, Rated Battlegrounds, or Solo Shuffle) during the active season by spending **Conquest, purchased from Zo'sorg in Oribos** at the seasonal PvP quartermaster.

### Farming Logistics
PvP appearances are tied to the season they were released in. The base appearance is available at low rating tiers (Combatant / Rival, around 1400 rating). If the original season is over, the appearance is no longer purchasable through normal means — check the **Black Market Auction House** (currently in Valdrakken) which occasionally lists legacy PvP pieces.

### Farming Tips
Wearable by any class — appearance is shared across all armor types. If you collected the set during its active season it is permanently unlocked in your Wardrobe even if the vendor no longer sells it. Check your Wardrobe before grinding — appearances persist across characters on the same account.

11919.content:
### How to Obtain
Drops from bosses inside **Castle Nathria** (Shadowlands). Each main armor slot (Head, Shoulders, Chest, Hands, Legs, Feet, Waist, Wrists) drops from a specific boss in the raid — open the set on Wowhead via the link below for the exact boss-to-slot mapping.

### Farming Logistics
Standard raid weekly lockout — the full instance resets every Tuesday. This is the Mythic difficulty version of the appearance. Other difficulties (LFR / Normal / Heroic / Mythic) use separate tints and are tracked as separate sets. At current max level the raid is usually soloable or trivially small-group, so you can collect missing pieces 1–2 weeks at a time.

### Farming Tips
Wearable by any class — appearance is shared across all armor types. Use the Great Vault and any bonus-roll currency from this expansion to maximize attempts per week. If a piece keeps not dropping, target-farm the specific boss and skip the rest.

11972.content:
### How to Obtain
PvP appearance from Battle for Azeroth. Earned through Rated PvP (Arenas, Rated Battlegrounds, or Solo Shuffle) during the active season by spending **Conquest and Marks of Honor** at the seasonal PvP quartermaster.

### Farming Logistics
PvP appearances are tied to the season they were released in. The base appearance is available at low rating tiers (Combatant / Rival, around 1400 rating). If the original season is over, the appearance is no longer purchasable through normal means — check the **Black Market Auction House** (currently in Valdrakken) which occasionally lists legacy PvP pieces.

### Farming Tips
Wearable by any class — appearance is shared across all armor types. If you collected the set during its active season it is permanently unlocked in your Wardrobe even if the vendor no longer sells it. Check your Wardrobe before grinding — appearances persist across characters on the same account.

11976.content:
### How to Obtain
**Mechagnome Heritage Armor** — earned by leveling a Mechagnome character to max level and completing the Mechagnome Heritage Armor questline, which unlocks from the racial leader once you hit cap.

### Farming Logistics
One-time per Mechagnome character. The questline is short (15–30 minutes) but requires the level cap, so the real time investment is leveling. Heritage Armor cannot be earned via Boosts and requires a "from-scratch" leveling experience on some races.

### Farming Tips
Wearable by any class — appearance is shared across all armor types. Heritage Armor is locked to the Mechagnome race specifically. The appearance is account-wide once unlocked — you can transmog it on any character of any class that race can play.

11977.content:
### How to Obtain
Vulpera Heritage Set is a Battle for Azeroth appearance set. The exact source isn't categorized in our database — open the Wowhead link below for the canonical piece list and drop locations.

### Farming Logistics
Wearable by any class — appearance is shared across all armor types. Most legacy sets at current max level are soloable; modern current-expansion sets may require active content engagement. Check the Wowhead set page for difficulty and source details.

### Farming Tips
If pieces are bind-on-equip, check the Auction House on your realm before farming directly. The Wardrobe automatically learns appearances on first loot pickup, so it's safe to roll Need even if you'll vendor the item later.

11996.content:
### How to Obtain
**Venthyr Covenant** cosmetic set from Shadowlands, earned in **Revendreth**. Pieces unlock as part of the covenant campaign through Renown levels — pledge to Venthyr and progress the campaign chapters.

### Farming Logistics
Renown progresses via Covenant Callings (3 world quests per day in your covenant's zone), the campaign chapter quests, and Anima-spending activities. Returning players benefit from catch-up — full Renown can be earned in a few sessions rather than weeks.

### Farming Tips
Wearable by any class — appearance is shared across all armor types. You must be pledged to Venthyr specifically; the set is not available cross-covenant. The Revendreth zone is also the source of mounts, pets, and other Venthyr-themed cosmetics worth picking up at the same time.

12002.content:
### How to Obtain
World-drop appearance from **Shadowlands** content. Pieces drop from outdoor mobs, rare elites, and treasure chests across the expansion's zones. This is a recolor / lookalike variant — the items may share appearances with a base set from a different expansion, so they can drop in zones outside the listed expansion range.

### Farming Logistics
No lockout — farm continuously. Individual drop rates from any specific mob are low, so most collectors check the **Auction House** first (these pieces are bind-on-equip and often listed). Rare-elite hunts on Time-walking weeks or Cross-Realm group routes massively speed up the search.

### Farming Tips
Wearable by any class — appearance is shared across all armor types. Install Handynotes plus the relevant expansion's "Rares + Treasures" plugin to map every spawn. AoE classes (Frost Mage, Havoc DH, Outlaw Rogue) clear contested zones faster than single-target builds.

12008.content:
### How to Obtain
Drops from bosses inside **Sanctum of Domination** (Shadowlands). Each main armor slot (Head, Shoulders, Chest, Hands, Legs, Feet, Waist, Wrists) drops from a specific boss in the raid — open the set on Wowhead via the link below for the exact boss-to-slot mapping.

### Farming Logistics
Standard raid weekly lockout — the full instance resets every Tuesday. This is the LFR difficulty version of the appearance. Other difficulties (LFR / Normal / Heroic / Mythic) use separate tints and are tracked as separate sets. At current max level the raid is usually soloable or trivially small-group, so you can collect missing pieces 1–2 weeks at a time.

### Farming Tips
Wearable by any class — appearance is shared across all armor types. Use the Great Vault and any bonus-roll currency from this expansion to maximize attempts per week. If a piece keeps not dropping, target-farm the specific boss and skip the rest.

12038.content:
### How to Obtain
PvP appearance from Shadowlands. Earned through Rated PvP (Arenas, Rated Battlegrounds, or Solo Shuffle) during the active season by spending **Conquest, purchased from Zo'sorg in Oribos** at the seasonal PvP quartermaster.

### Farming Logistics
PvP appearances are tied to the season they were released in. The base appearance is available at low rating tiers (Combatant / Rival, around 1400 rating). If the original season is over, the appearance is no longer purchasable through normal means — check the **Black Market Auction House** (currently in Valdrakken) which occasionally lists legacy PvP pieces.

### Farming Tips
Wearable by any class — appearance is shared across all armor types. If you collected the set during its active season it is permanently unlocked in your Wardrobe even if the vendor no longer sells it. Check your Wardrobe before grinding — appearances persist across characters on the same account.

12057.content:
### How to Obtain
Drops from bosses inside **Sepulcher of the First Ones** (Shadowlands). Each main armor slot (Head, Shoulders, Chest, Hands, Legs, Feet, Waist, Wrists) drops from a specific boss in the raid — open the set on Wowhead via the link below for the exact boss-to-slot mapping.

### Farming Logistics
Standard raid weekly lockout — the full instance resets every Tuesday. This is the Normal difficulty version of the appearance. Other difficulties (LFR / Normal / Heroic / Mythic) use separate tints and are tracked as separate sets. At current max level the raid is usually soloable or trivially small-group, so you can collect missing pieces 1–2 weeks at a time.

### Farming Tips
Plate armor, available to Paladin. Use the Great Vault and any bonus-roll currency from this expansion to maximize attempts per week. If a piece keeps not dropping, target-farm the specific boss and skip the rest.

Standard raid weekly lockout — the full instance resets every Tuesday. This is the Heroic difficulty version of the appearance. Other difficulties (LFR / Normal / Heroic / Mythic) use separate tints and are tracked as separate sets. At current max level the raid is usually soloable or trivially small-group, so you can collect missing pieces 1–2 weeks at a time.

### Farming Tips
Mail armor, available to Shaman. Use the Great Vault and any bonus-roll currency from this expansion to maximize attempts per week. If a piece keeps not dropping, target-farm the specific boss and skip the rest.

12106.content:
### How to Obtain
PvP appearance from Shadowlands. Earned through Rated PvP (Arenas, Rated Battlegrounds, or Solo Shuffle) during the active season by spending **Conquest, purchased from Zo'sorg in Oribos** at the seasonal PvP quartermaster.

### Farming Logistics
PvP appearances are tied to the season they were released in. The base appearance is available at low rating tiers (Combatant / Rival, around 1400 rating). If the original season is over, the appearance is no longer purchasable through normal means — check the **Black Market Auction House** (currently in Valdrakken) which occasionally lists legacy PvP pieces.

### Farming Tips
Mail armor, available to Hunter. If you collected the set during its active season it is permanently unlocked in your Wardrobe even if the vendor no longer sells it. Check your Wardrobe before grinding — appearances persist across characters on the same account.

12122.content:
### How to Obtain
Available from the **Trading Post**, located in Orgrimmar (Valley of Strength) or Stormwind (Trade District). Purchased with **Trader's Tender**, the currency earned by completing monthly Traveler's Log activities (about 500 Tender per month plus bonus activities).

### Farming Logistics
Trading Post inventory rotates **monthly** — if this set is not in the current month's catalog, it will eventually return in a future rotation. Tender carries over indefinitely between months, so you can save up for higher-priced items. No lockout once purchased.

### Farming Tips
Wearable by any class — appearance is shared across all armor types. Complete every Traveler's Log activity each month to maximize Tender — they cover all content (dungeons, raids, PvP, world quests, professions). Set bundles typically cost 500–750 Tender; individual pieces cost less.

Tattered Cloth Regalia is a Shadowlands appearance set. The exact source isn't categorized in our database — open the Wowhead link below for the canonical piece list and drop locations.

### Farming Logistics
Wearable by any class — appearance is shared across all armor types. Most legacy sets at current max level are soloable; modern current-expansion sets may require active content engagement. Check the Wowhead set page for difficulty and source details.

### Farming Tips
If pieces are bind-on-equip, check the Auction House on your realm before farming directly. The Wardrobe automatically learns appearances on first loot pickup, so it's safe to roll Need even if you'll vendor the item later.

12128.content:
### How to Obtain
Crafted by **Tailoring** in Shadowlands. Patterns are obtained from profession trainers, world drops, or rare recipes from the expansion's content. Each piece requires expansion-tier materials that you can either farm yourself or buy from the Auction House.

### Farming Logistics
No lockout — craft as many pieces as you have materials for. Profession Crafting Orders (introduced in Dragonflight) let you commission a crafter via the public order board without leveling the profession yourself, so a non-crafter alt can still obtain the appearance.

### Farming Tips
Wearable by any class — appearance is shared across all armor types. Check the Auction House before crafting — many crafted appearances are bind-on-equip and other players list them directly. If you need to power-level the profession to craft this set, prioritize gathering professions for materials first.

12139.content:
### How to Obtain
Available from the **Trading Post**, located in Orgrimmar (Valley of Strength) or Stormwind (Trade District). Purchased with **Trader's Tender**, the currency earned by completing monthly Traveler's Log activities (about 500 Tender per month plus bonus activities).

### Farming Logistics
Trading Post inventory rotates **monthly** — if this set is not in the current month's catalog, it will eventually return in a future rotation. Tender carries over indefinitely between months, so you can save up for higher-priced items. No lockout once purchased.

### Farming Tips
Wearable by any class — appearance is shared across all armor types. Complete every Traveler's Log activity each month to maximize Tender — they cover all content (dungeons, raids, PvP, world quests, professions). Set bundles typically cost 500–750 Tender; individual pieces cost less.

12172.content:
### How to Obtain
World-drop appearance from **Shadowlands** content. Pieces drop from outdoor mobs, rare elites, and treasure chests across the expansion's zones. This is a recolor / lookalike variant — the items may share appearances with a base set from a different expansion, so they can drop in zones outside the listed expansion range.

### Farming Logistics
No lockout — farm continuously. Individual drop rates from any specific mob are low, so most collectors check the **Auction House** first (these pieces are bind-on-equip and often listed). Rare-elite hunts on Time-walking weeks or Cross-Realm group routes massively speed up the search.

### Farming Tips
Wearable by any class — appearance is shared across all armor types. Install Handynotes plus the relevant expansion's "Rares + Treasures" plugin to map every spawn. AoE classes (Frost Mage, Havoc DH, Outlaw Rogue) clear contested zones faster than single-target builds.

No lockout — farm continuously. Individual drop rates from any specific mob are low, so most collectors check the **Auction House** first (these pieces are bind-on-equip and often listed). Rare-elite hunts on Time-walking weeks or Cross-Realm group routes massively speed up the search.

### Farming Tips
Wearable by any class — appearance is shared across all armor types. Install Handynotes plus the relevant expansion's "Rares + Treasures" plugin to map every spawn. AoE classes (Frost Mage, Havoc DH, Outlaw Rogue) clear contested zones faster than single-target builds.

12190.content:
### How to Obtain
Drops from bosses inside **a Shadowlands-era raid** (Shadowlands). Each main armor slot (Head, Shoulders, Chest, Hands, Legs, Feet, Waist, Wrists) drops from a specific boss in the raid — open the set on Wowhead via the link below for the exact boss-to-slot mapping.

### Farming Logistics
Standard raid weekly lockout — the full instance resets every Tuesday. This is the Heroic difficulty version of the appearance. Other difficulties (LFR / Normal / Heroic / Mythic) use separate tints and are tracked as separate sets. At current max level the raid is usually soloable or trivially small-group, so you can collect missing pieces 1–2 weeks at a time.

### Farming Tips
Wearable by any class — appearance is shared across all armor types. Use the Great Vault and any bonus-roll currency from this expansion to maximize attempts per week. If a piece keeps not dropping, target-farm the specific boss and skip the rest.

Leather armor, available to Rogue. Use the Great Vault and any bonus-roll currency from this expansion to maximize attempts per week. If a piece keeps not dropping, target-farm the specific boss and skip the rest.

12240.content:
### How to Obtain
PvP appearance from Dragonflight. Earned through Rated PvP (Arenas, Rated Battlegrounds, or Solo Shuffle) during the active season by spending **Conquest, purchased from Aspirant's Quartermaster in Valdrakken** at the seasonal PvP quartermaster.

### Farming Logistics
PvP appearances are tied to the season they were released in. The base appearance is available at low rating tiers (Combatant / Rival, around 1400 rating). If the original season is over, the appearance is no longer purchasable through normal means — check the **Black Market Auction House** (currently in Valdrakken) which occasionally lists legacy PvP pieces.

### Farming Tips
Cloth armor, available to Priest. If you collected the set during its active season it is permanently unlocked in your Wardrobe even if the vendor no longer sells it. Check your Wardrobe before grinding — appearances persist across characters on the same account.

12241.content:
### How to Obtain
PvP appearance from Dragonflight. Earned through Rated PvP (Arenas, Rated Battlegrounds, or Solo Shuffle) during the active season by spending **Conquest, purchased from Aspirant's Quartermaster in Valdrakken** at the seasonal PvP quartermaster.

### Farming Logistics
PvP appearances are tied to the season they were released in. This is the Elite recolor variant — it requires a higher Rated PvP threshold (typically Duelist 2100+ or Gladiator 2400+) to purchase, earned during the original season. If the original season is over, the appearance is no longer purchasable through normal means — check the **Black Market Auction House** (currently in Valdrakken) which occasionally lists legacy PvP pieces.

PvP appearance from Dragonflight. Earned through Rated PvP (Arenas, Rated Battlegrounds, or Solo Shuffle) during the active season by spending **Conquest, purchased from Aspirant's Quartermaster in Valdrakken** at the seasonal PvP quartermaster.

### Farming Logistics
PvP appearances are tied to the season they were released in. This is the Elite recolor variant — it requires a higher Rated PvP threshold (typically Duelist 2100+ or Gladiator 2400+) to purchase, earned during the original season. If the original season is over, the appearance is no longer purchasable through normal means — check the **Black Market Auction House** (currently in Valdrakken) which occasionally lists legacy PvP pieces.

### Farming Tips
Leather armor, available to Druid. If you collected the set during its active season it is permanently unlocked in your Wardrobe even if the vendor no longer sells it. Check your Wardrobe before grinding — appearances persist across characters on the same account.

12266.content:
### How to Obtain
Drops from bosses inside **Aberrus** (Dragonflight). Each main armor slot (Head, Shoulders, Chest, Hands, Legs, Feet, Waist, Wrists) drops from a specific boss in the raid — open the set on Wowhead via the link below for the exact boss-to-slot mapping.

### Farming Logistics
Standard raid weekly lockout — the full instance resets every Tuesday. This is the LFR difficulty version of the appearance. Other difficulties (LFR / Normal / Heroic / Mythic) use separate tints and are tracked as separate sets. At current max level the raid is usually soloable or trivially small-group, so you can collect missing pieces 1–2 weeks at a time.

### Farming Tips
Cloth armor, available to Priest. Use the Great Vault and any bonus-roll currency from this expansion to maximize attempts per week. If a piece keeps not dropping, target-farm the specific boss and skip the rest.

PvP appearance from Dragonflight. Earned through Rated PvP (Arenas, Rated Battlegrounds, or Solo Shuffle) during the active season by spending **Conquest, purchased from Aspirant's Quartermaster in Valdrakken** at the seasonal PvP quartermaster.

### Farming Logistics
PvP appearances are tied to the season they were released in. The base appearance is available at low rating tiers (Combatant / Rival, around 1400 rating). If the original season is over, the appearance is no longer purchasable through normal means — check the **Black Market Auction House** (currently in Valdrakken) which occasionally lists legacy PvP pieces.

### Farming Tips
Cloth armor, available to Mage. If you collected the set during its active season it is permanently unlocked in your Wardrobe even if the vendor no longer sells it. Check your Wardrobe before grinding — appearances persist across characters on the same account.

12331.content:
### How to Obtain
Primal Storms Cloth Set is a Dragonflight appearance set. The exact source isn't categorized in our database — open the Wowhead link below for the canonical piece list and drop locations.

### Farming Logistics
Wearable by any class — appearance is shared across all armor types. Most legacy sets at current max level are soloable; modern current-expansion sets may require active content engagement. Check the Wowhead set page for difficulty and source details.

### Farming Tips
If pieces are bind-on-equip, check the Auction House on your realm before farming directly. The Wardrobe automatically learns appearances on first loot pickup, so it's safe to roll Need even if you'll vendor the item later.

12332.content:
### How to Obtain
World-drop appearance from **Dragonflight** content. Pieces drop from outdoor mobs, rare elites, and treasure chests across the expansion's zones. This is a recolor / lookalike variant — the items may share appearances with a base set from a different expansion, so they can drop in zones outside the listed expansion range.

### Farming Logistics
No lockout — farm continuously. Individual drop rates from any specific mob are low, so most collectors check the **Auction House** first (these pieces are bind-on-equip and often listed). Rare-elite hunts on Time-walking weeks or Cross-Realm group routes massively speed up the search.

### Farming Tips
Wearable by any class — appearance is shared across all armor types. Install Handynotes plus the relevant expansion's "Rares + Treasures" plugin to map every spawn. AoE classes (Frost Mage, Havoc DH, Outlaw Rogue) clear contested zones faster than single-target builds.

12341.content:
### How to Obtain
Available from the **Trading Post**, located in Orgrimmar (Valley of Strength) or Stormwind (Trade District). Purchased with **Trader's Tender**, the currency earned by completing monthly Traveler's Log activities (about 500 Tender per month plus bonus activities).

### Farming Logistics
Trading Post inventory rotates **monthly** — if this set is not in the current month's catalog, it will eventually return in a future rotation. Tender carries over indefinitely between months, so you can save up for higher-priced items. No lockout once purchased.

### Farming Tips
Wearable by any class — appearance is shared across all armor types. Complete every Traveler's Log activity each month to maximize Tender — they cover all content (dungeons, raids, PvP, world quests, professions). Set bundles typically cost 500–750 Tender; individual pieces cost less.

12350.content:
### How to Obtain
Drops from bosses inside **Nerub-ar Palace** (The War Within). Each main armor slot (Head, Shoulders, Chest, Hands, Legs, Feet, Waist, Wrists) drops from a specific boss in the raid — open the set on Wowhead via the link below for the exact boss-to-slot mapping.

### Farming Logistics
Standard raid weekly lockout — the full instance resets every Tuesday. This is the Heroic difficulty version of the appearance. Other difficulties (LFR / Normal / Heroic / Mythic) use separate tints and are tracked as separate sets. At current max level the raid is usually soloable or trivially small-group, so you can collect missing pieces 1–2 weeks at a time.

### Farming Tips
Leather, Mail armor, available to Hunter, Demon Hunter. Use the Great Vault and any bonus-roll currency from this expansion to maximize attempts per week. If a piece keeps not dropping, target-farm the specific boss and skip the rest.

12383.content:
### How to Obtain
Drops from bosses inside **Nerub-ar Palace** (The War Within). Each main armor slot (Head, Shoulders, Chest, Hands, Legs, Feet, Waist, Wrists) drops from a specific boss in the raid — open the set on Wowhead via the link below for the exact boss-to-slot mapping.

### Farming Logistics
Standard raid weekly lockout — the full instance resets every Tuesday. This is the Normal difficulty version of the appearance. Other difficulties (LFR / Normal / Heroic / Mythic) use separate tints and are tracked as separate sets. At current max level the raid is usually soloable or trivially small-group, so you can collect missing pieces 1–2 weeks at a time.

### Farming Tips
Leather, Mail armor, available to Hunter, Demon Hunter. Use the Great Vault and any bonus-roll currency from this expansion to maximize attempts per week. If a piece keeps not dropping, target-farm the specific boss and skip the rest.

Drops from bosses inside **Nerub-ar Palace** (The War Within). Each main armor slot (Head, Shoulders, Chest, Hands, Legs, Feet, Waist, Wrists) drops from a specific boss in the raid — open the set on Wowhead via the link below for the exact boss-to-slot mapping.

### Farming Logistics
Standard raid weekly lockout — the full instance resets every Tuesday. This is the LFR difficulty version of the appearance. Other difficulties (LFR / Normal / Heroic / Mythic) use separate tints and are tracked as separate sets. At current max level the raid is usually soloable or trivially small-group, so you can collect missing pieces 1–2 weeks at a time.

### Farming Tips
Mail armor, available to Evoker. Use the Great Vault and any bonus-roll currency from this expansion to maximize attempts per week. If a piece keeps not dropping, target-farm the specific boss and skip the rest.

12401.content:
### How to Obtain
PvP appearance from The War Within. Earned through Rated PvP (Arenas, Rated Battlegrounds, or Solo Shuffle) during the active season by spending **Conquest, purchased from the PvP vendor in Dornogal** at the seasonal PvP quartermaster.

### Farming Logistics
PvP appearances are tied to the season they were released in. The base appearance is available at low rating tiers (Combatant / Rival, around 1400 rating). If the original season is over, the appearance is no longer purchasable through normal means — check the **Black Market Auction House** (currently in Valdrakken) which occasionally lists legacy PvP pieces.

### Farming Tips
Mail armor, available to Evoker. If you collected the set during its active season it is permanently unlocked in your Wardrobe even if the vendor no longer sells it. Check your Wardrobe before grinding — appearances persist across characters on the same account.

12448.content:
### How to Obtain
Crafted by **Blacksmithing** in The War Within. Patterns are obtained from profession trainers, world drops, or rare recipes from the expansion's content. Each piece requires expansion-tier materials that you can either farm yourself or buy from the Auction House.

### Farming Logistics
No lockout — craft as many pieces as you have materials for. Profession Crafting Orders (introduced in Dragonflight) let you commission a crafter via the public order board without leveling the profession yourself, so a non-crafter alt can still obtain the appearance.

### Farming Tips
Wearable by any class — appearance is shared across all armor types. Check the Auction House before crafting — many crafted appearances are bind-on-equip and other players list them directly. If you need to power-level the profession to craft this set, prioritize gathering professions for materials first.

12452.content:
### How to Obtain
Cave Topographer's Cloth Set is a The War Within appearance set. The exact source isn't categorized in our database — open the Wowhead link below for the canonical piece list and drop locations.

### Farming Logistics
Wearable by any class — appearance is shared across all armor types. Most legacy sets at current max level are soloable; modern current-expansion sets may require active content engagement. Check the Wowhead set page for difficulty and source details.

### Farming Tips
If pieces are bind-on-equip, check the Auction House on your realm before farming directly. The Wardrobe automatically learns appearances on first loot pickup, so it's safe to roll Need even if you'll vendor the item later.

12453.content:
### How to Obtain
**Earthen Heritage Armor** — earned by leveling a Earthen character to max level and completing the Earthen Heritage Armor questline, which unlocks from the racial leader once you hit cap.

### Farming Logistics
One-time per Earthen character. The questline is short (15–30 minutes) but requires the level cap, so the real time investment is leveling. Heritage Armor cannot be earned via Boosts and requires a "from-scratch" leveling experience on some races.

### Farming Tips
Wearable by any class — appearance is shared across all armor types. Heritage Armor is locked to the Earthen race specifically. The appearance is account-wide once unlocked — you can transmog it on any character of any class that race can play.

12464.content:
### How to Obtain
Drops from dungeon bosses in The War Within-era dungeons. Set pieces are spread across multiple dungeons in the expansion — open the Wowhead link below to see the exact dungeon and boss for each slot.

### Farming Logistics
Dungeons have a per-character daily reset rather than a weekly lockout, so you can rerun the same dungeon repeatedly for missing pieces. At current max level the content is fully soloable in 5–10 minutes per run. Drop rates per relevant boss are typically 15–25 %.

### Farming Tips
Wearable by any class — appearance is shared across all armor types. Mythic+ does not change the appearance — running on Normal is the fastest way to farm. Pick a class with strong AoE for speed; alts with the same armor type can funnel duplicate drops.

12477.content:
### How to Obtain
PvP appearance from The War Within. Earned through Rated PvP (Arenas, Rated Battlegrounds, or Solo Shuffle) during the active season by spending **Conquest, purchased from the PvP vendor in Dornogal** at the seasonal PvP quartermaster.

### Farming Logistics
PvP appearances are tied to the season they were released in. The base appearance is available at low rating tiers (Combatant / Rival, around 1400 rating). If the original season is over, the appearance is no longer purchasable through normal means — check the **Black Market Auction House** (currently in Valdrakken) which occasionally lists legacy PvP pieces.

### Farming Tips
Wearable by any class — appearance is shared across all armor types. If you collected the set during its active season it is permanently unlocked in your Wardrobe even if the vendor no longer sells it. Check your Wardrobe before grinding — appearances persist across characters on the same account.

12499.content:
### How to Obtain
Available from the **Trading Post**, located in Orgrimmar (Valley of Strength) or Stormwind (Trade District). Purchased with **Trader's Tender**, the currency earned by completing monthly Traveler's Log activities (about 500 Tender per month plus bonus activities).

### Farming Logistics
Trading Post inventory rotates **monthly** — if this set is not in the current month's catalog, it will eventually return in a future rotation. Tender carries over indefinitely between months, so you can save up for higher-priced items. No lockout once purchased.

### Farming Tips
Wearable by any class — appearance is shared across all armor types. Complete every Traveler's Log activity each month to maximize Tender — they cover all content (dungeons, raids, PvP, world quests, professions). Set bundles typically cost 500–750 Tender; individual pieces cost less.

12518.content:
### How to Obtain
PvP appearance from The War Within. Earned through Rated PvP (Arenas, Rated Battlegrounds, or Solo Shuffle) during the active season by spending **Conquest, purchased from the PvP vendor in Dornogal** at the seasonal PvP quartermaster.

### Farming Logistics
PvP appearances are tied to the season they were released in. The base appearance is available at low rating tiers (Combatant / Rival, around 1400 rating). If the original season is over, the appearance is no longer purchasable through normal means — check the **Black Market Auction House** (currently in Valdrakken) which occasionally lists legacy PvP pieces.

### Farming Tips
Wearable by any class — appearance is shared across all armor types. If you collected the set during its active season it is permanently unlocked in your Wardrobe even if the vendor no longer sells it. Check your Wardrobe before grinding — appearances persist across characters on the same account.

12525.content:
### How to Obtain
Available from the **Trading Post**, located in Orgrimmar (Valley of Strength) or Stormwind (Trade District). Purchased with **Trader's Tender**, the currency earned by completing monthly Traveler's Log activities (about 500 Tender per month plus bonus activities).

### Farming Logistics
Trading Post inventory rotates **monthly** — if this set is not in the current month's catalog, it will eventually return in a future rotation. Tender carries over indefinitely between months, so you can save up for higher-priced items. No lockout once purchased.

### Farming Tips
Wearable by any class — appearance is shared across all armor types. Complete every Traveler's Log activity each month to maximize Tender — they cover all content (dungeons, raids, PvP, world quests, professions). Set bundles typically cost 500–750 Tender; individual pieces cost less.

12543.content:
### How to Obtain
Crafted by **a crafting profession** in The War Within. Patterns are obtained from profession trainers, world drops, or rare recipes from the expansion's content. Each piece requires expansion-tier materials that you can either farm yourself or buy from the Auction House.

### Farming Logistics
No lockout — craft as many pieces as you have materials for. Profession Crafting Orders (introduced in Dragonflight) let you commission a crafter via the public order board without leveling the profession yourself, so a non-crafter alt can still obtain the appearance.

### Farming Tips
Wearable by any class — appearance is shared across all armor types. Check the Auction House before crafting — many crafted appearances are bind-on-equip and other players list them directly. If you need to power-level the profession to craft this set, prioritize gathering professions for materials first.

Trading Post inventory rotates **monthly** — if this set is not in the current month's catalog, it will eventually return in a future rotation. Tender carries over indefinitely between months, so you can save up for higher-priced items. No lockout once purchased.

### Farming Tips
Cloth armor, available to Priest. Complete every Traveler's Log activity each month to maximize Tender — they cover all content (dungeons, raids, PvP, world quests, professions). Set bundles typically cost 500–750 Tender; individual pieces cost less.

12570.content:
### How to Obtain
PvP appearance from The War Within. Earned through Rated PvP (Arenas, Rated Battlegrounds, or Solo Shuffle) during the active season by spending **Conquest, purchased from the PvP vendor in Dornogal** at the seasonal PvP quartermaster.

### Farming Logistics
PvP appearances are tied to the season they were released in. The base appearance is available at low rating tiers (Combatant / Rival, around 1400 rating). If the original season is over, the appearance is no longer purchasable through normal means — check the **Black Market Auction House** (currently in Valdrakken) which occasionally lists legacy PvP pieces.

### Farming Tips
Wearable by any class — appearance is shared across all armor types. If you collected the set during its active season it is permanently unlocked in your Wardrobe even if the vendor no longer sells it. Check your Wardrobe before grinding — appearances persist across characters on the same account.

12619.content:
### How to Obtain
Available from the **Trading Post**, located in Orgrimmar (Valley of Strength) or Stormwind (Trade District). Purchased with **Trader's Tender**, the currency earned by completing monthly Traveler's Log activities (about 500 Tender per month plus bonus activities).

### Farming Logistics
Trading Post inventory rotates **monthly** — if this set is not in the current month's catalog, it will eventually return in a future rotation. Tender carries over indefinitely between months, so you can save up for higher-priced items. No lockout once purchased.

### Farming Tips
Wearable by any class — appearance is shared across all armor types. Complete every Traveler's Log activity each month to maximize Tender — they cover all content (dungeons, raids, PvP, world quests, professions). Set bundles typically cost 500–750 Tender; individual pieces cost less.

12646.content:
### How to Obtain
Available from the **Trading Post**, located in Orgrimmar (Valley of Strength) or Stormwind (Trade District). Purchased with **Trader's Tender**, the currency earned by completing monthly Traveler's Log activities (about 500 Tender per month plus bonus activities).

### Farming Logistics
Trading Post inventory rotates **monthly** — if this set is not in the current month's catalog, it will eventually return in a future rotation. Tender carries over indefinitely between months, so you can save up for higher-priced items. No lockout once purchased.

### Farming Tips
Wearable by any class — appearance is shared across all armor types. Complete every Traveler's Log activity each month to maximize Tender — they cover all content (dungeons, raids, PvP, world quests, professions). Set bundles typically cost 500–750 Tender; individual pieces cost less.

12685.content:
### How to Obtain
**Troll Heritage Armor** — earned by leveling a Troll character to max level and completing the Troll Heritage Armor questline, which unlocks from the racial leader once you hit cap.

### Farming Logistics
One-time per Troll character. The questline is short (15–30 minutes) but requires the level cap, so the real time investment is leveling. Heritage Armor cannot be earned via Boosts and requires a "from-scratch" leveling experience on some races.

### Farming Tips
Wearable by any class — appearance is shared across all armor types. Heritage Armor is locked to the Troll race specifically. The appearance is account-wide once unlocked — you can transmog it on any character of any class that race can play.

Trading Post inventory rotates **monthly** — if this set is not in the current month's catalog, it will eventually return in a future rotation. Tender carries over indefinitely between months, so you can save up for higher-priced items. No lockout once purchased.

### Farming Tips
Wearable by any class — appearance is shared across all armor types. Complete every Traveler's Log activity each month to maximize Tender — they cover all content (dungeons, raids, PvP, world quests, professions). Set bundles typically cost 500–750 Tender; individual pieces cost less.

12698.content:
### How to Obtain
Excavator's Set is a The War Within appearance set. The exact source isn't categorized in our database — open the Wowhead link below for the canonical piece list and drop locations.

### Farming Logistics
Wearable by any class — appearance is shared across all armor types. Most legacy sets at current max level are soloable; modern current-expansion sets may require active content engagement. Check the Wowhead set page for difficulty and source details.

### Farming Tips
If pieces are bind-on-equip, check the Auction House on your realm before farming directly. The Wardrobe automatically learns appearances on first loot pickup, so it's safe to roll Need even if you'll vendor the item later.

12700.content:
### How to Obtain
**Worgen Heritage Armor** — earned by leveling a Worgen character to max level and completing the Worgen Heritage Armor questline, which unlocks from the racial leader once you hit cap.

### Farming Logistics
One-time per Worgen character. The questline is short (15–30 minutes) but requires the level cap, so the real time investment is leveling. Heritage Armor cannot be earned via Boosts and requires a "from-scratch" leveling experience on some races.

### Farming Tips
Wearable by any class — appearance is shared across all armor types. Heritage Armor is locked to the Worgen race specifically. The appearance is account-wide once unlocked — you can transmog it on any character of any class that race can play.

12707.content:
### How to Obtain
Drops from bosses inside **Amirdrassil** (Dragonflight). Each main armor slot (Head, Shoulders, Chest, Hands, Legs, Feet, Waist, Wrists) drops from a specific boss in the raid — open the set on Wowhead via the link below for the exact boss-to-slot mapping.

### Farming Logistics
Standard raid weekly lockout — the full instance resets every Tuesday. This is the Normal difficulty version of the appearance. Other difficulties (LFR / Normal / Heroic / Mythic) use separate tints and are tracked as separate sets. At current max level the raid is usually soloable or trivially small-group, so you can collect missing pieces 1–2 weeks at a time.

### Farming Tips
Wearable by any class — appearance is shared across all armor types. Use the Great Vault and any bonus-roll currency from this expansion to maximize attempts per week. If a piece keeps not dropping, target-farm the specific boss and skip the rest.
