12711.content:
### How to Obtain
PvP appearance from Dragonflight. Earned through Rated PvP (Arenas, Rated Battlegrounds, or Solo Shuffle) during the active season by spending **Conquest, purchased from Aspirant's Quartermaster in Valdrakken** at the seasonal PvP quartermaster.

### Farming Logistics
PvP appearances are tied to the season they were released in. This is the Elite recolor variant — it requires a higher Rated PvP threshold (typically Duelist 2100+ or Gladiator 2400+) to purchase, earned during the original season. If the original season is over, the appearance is no longer purchasable through normal means — check the **Black Market Auction House** (currently in Valdrakken) which occasionally lists legacy PvP pieces.

### Farming Tips
Plate armor, available to Death Knight. If you collected the set during its active season it is permanently unlocked in your Wardrobe even if the vendor no longer sells it. Check your Wardrobe before grinding — appearances persist across characters on the same account.

PvP appearance from Dragonflight. Earned through Rated PvP (Arenas, Rated Battlegrounds, or Solo Shuffle) during the active season by spending **Conquest, purchased from Aspirant's Quartermaster in Valdrakken** at the seasonal PvP quartermaster.

### Farming Logistics
PvP appearances are tied to the season they were released in. This is the Elite recolor variant — it requires a higher Rated PvP threshold (typically Duelist 2100+ or Gladiator 2400+) to purchase, earned during the original season. If the original season is over, the appearance is no longer purchasable through normal means — check the **Black Market Auction House** (currently in Valdrakken) which occasionally lists legacy PvP pieces.

### Farming Tips
Cloth armor, available to Priest. If you collected the set during its active season it is permanently unlocked in your Wardrobe even if the vendor no longer sells it. Check your Wardrobe before grinding — appearances persist across characters on the same account.

12761.content:
### How to Obtain
Drops from bosses inside **Amirdrassil** (Dragonflight). Each main armor slot (Head, Shoulders, Chest, Hands, Legs, Feet, Waist, Wrists) drops from a specific boss in the raid — open the set on Wowhead via the link below for the exact boss-to-slot mapping.

### Farming Logistics
Standard raid weekly lockout — the full instance resets every Tuesday. This is the Normal difficulty version of the appearance. Other difficulties (LFR / Normal / Heroic / Mythic) use separate tints and are tracked as separate sets. At current max level the raid is usually soloable or trivially small-group, so you can collect missing pieces 1–2 weeks at a time.

### Farming Tips
Wearable by any class — appearance is shared across all armor types. Use the Great Vault and any bonus-roll currency from this expansion to maximize attempts per week. If a piece keeps not dropping, target-farm the specific boss and skip the rest.

12785.content:
### How to Obtain
Ceremonial Jacaranda Regalia: Emerald Bounties is a The War Within appearance set. The exact source isn't categorized in our database — open the Wowhead link below for the canonical piece list and drop locations.

### Farming Logistics
Wearable by any class — appearance is shared across all armor types. Most legacy sets at current max level are soloable; modern current-expansion sets may require active content engagement. Check the Wowhead set page for difficulty and source details.

### Farming Tips
If pieces are bind-on-equip, check the Auction House on your realm before farming directly. The Wardrobe automatically learns appearances on first loot pickup, so it's safe to roll Need even if you'll vendor the item later.

12801.content:
### How to Obtain
**Night Elf Heritage Armor** — earned by leveling a Night Elf character to max level and completing the Night Elf Heritage Armor questline, which unlocks from the racial leader once you hit cap.

### Farming Logistics
One-time per Night Elf character. The questline is short (15–30 minutes) but requires the level cap, so the real time investment is leveling. Heritage Armor cannot be earned via Boosts and requires a "from-scratch" leveling experience on some races.

### Farming Tips
Wearable by any class — appearance is shared across all armor types. Heritage Armor is locked to the Night Elf race specifically. The appearance is account-wide once unlocked — you can transmog it on any character of any class that race can play.

12804.content:
### How to Obtain
Heritage Armor — earned by leveling a character of the relevant allied or core race to max level and completing that race's Heritage Armor questline.

### Farming Logistics
One-time per character: complete the full Heritage Armor quest chain, available from the racial leader once your character of the correct race hits max level. No lockout, but it does require playing the full character to cap.

### Farming Tips
Wearable by any class — appearance is shared across all armor types. Heritage Armor is faction-locked to the race that earns it. Use Recruit-a-Friend boosts or experience buffs to speed up the level grind if needed.

12847.content:
### How to Obtain
Available from the **Trading Post**, located in Orgrimmar (Valley of Strength) or Stormwind (Trade District). Purchased with **Trader's Tender**, the currency earned by completing monthly Traveler's Log activities (about 500 Tender per month plus bonus activities).

### Farming Logistics
Trading Post inventory rotates **monthly** — if this set is not in the current month's catalog, it will eventually return in a future rotation. Tender carries over indefinitely between months, so you can save up for higher-priced items. No lockout once purchased.

### Farming Tips
Wearable by any class — appearance is shared across all armor types. Complete every Traveler's Log activity each month to maximize Tender — they cover all content (dungeons, raids, PvP, world quests, professions). Set bundles typically cost 500–750 Tender; individual pieces cost less.

12883.content:
### How to Obtain
PvP appearance from The War Within. Earned through Rated PvP (Arenas, Rated Battlegrounds, or Solo Shuffle) during the active season by spending **Conquest, purchased from the PvP vendor in Dornogal** at the seasonal PvP quartermaster.

### Farming Logistics
PvP appearances are tied to the season they were released in. The base appearance is available at low rating tiers (Combatant / Rival, around 1400 rating). If the original season is over, the appearance is no longer purchasable through normal means — check the **Black Market Auction House** (currently in Valdrakken) which occasionally lists legacy PvP pieces.

### Farming Tips
Wearable by any class — appearance is shared across all armor types. If you collected the set during its active season it is permanently unlocked in your Wardrobe even if the vendor no longer sells it. Check your Wardrobe before grinding — appearances persist across characters on the same account.

12891.content:
### How to Obtain
PvP appearance from The War Within. Earned through Rated PvP (Arenas, Rated Battlegrounds, or Solo Shuffle) during the active season by spending **Conquest, purchased from the PvP vendor in Dornogal** at the seasonal PvP quartermaster.

### Farming Logistics
PvP appearances are tied to the season they were released in. The base appearance is available at low rating tiers (Combatant / Rival, around 1400 rating). If the original season is over, the appearance is no longer purchasable through normal means — check the **Black Market Auction House** (currently in Valdrakken) which occasionally lists legacy PvP pieces.

### Farming Tips
Wearable by any class — appearance is shared across all armor types. If you collected the set during its active season it is permanently unlocked in your Wardrobe even if the vendor no longer sells it. Check your Wardrobe before grinding — appearances persist across characters on the same account.

12923.content:
### How to Obtain
World-drop appearance from **The War Within** content. Pieces drop from outdoor mobs, rare elites, and treasure chests across the expansion's zones. This is a recolor / lookalike variant — the items may share appearances with a base set from a different expansion, so they can drop in zones outside the listed expansion range.

### Farming Logistics
No lockout — farm continuously. Individual drop rates from any specific mob are low, so most collectors check the **Auction House** first (these pieces are bind-on-equip and often listed). Rare-elite hunts on Time-walking weeks or Cross-Realm group routes massively speed up the search.

### Farming Tips
Wearable by any class — appearance is shared across all armor types. Install Handynotes plus the relevant expansion's "Rares + Treasures" plugin to map every spawn. AoE classes (Frost Mage, Havoc DH, Outlaw Rogue) clear contested zones faster than single-target builds.
